s1.title: Mool Mantar
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਕਰਤਾ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਨਿਰਭਉ ਨਿਰਵੈਰੁ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This compresses Sikh theology into a single formula: reality, truth, creativity, fearlessness, and timelessness. A learner gains core vocabulary and a conceptual map that reappears across Gurbani, kirtan, and everyday Sikh speech.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / first line taught to children / widely quoted in diaspora
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ੴ ਸਤਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਕਰਤਾ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਨਿਰਭਉ ਨਿਰਵੈਰੁ
ਅਕਾਲ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਅਜੂਨੀ ਸੈਭੰ ਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
॥ ਜਪੁ ॥
ਆਦਿ ਸਚੁ ਜੁਗਾਦਿ ਸਚੁ ॥
ਹੈ ਭੀ ਸਚੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਹੋਸੀ ਭੀ ਸਚੁ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
Ik Oankar sat naam kartaa purakh nirbhau nirvair
Akaal moorat ajoonee saibhang gur prasaad.
Jap.
Aad sach jugaad sach.
Hai bhee sach Nanak hosee bhee sach. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: One Universal Reality: Truth is the Name; the Creator Being, without fear, without enmity. Timeless in form, unborn, self-existent, known by the Guru’s grace. Recite: True in the beginning, true through the ages; true now as well, O Nanak, and true shall also be.

s1.title: Japji Pauri 1
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਸੋਚੈ ਸੋਚਿ ਨ ਹੋਵਈ ਜੇ ਸੋਚੀ ਲਖ ਵਾਰ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This teaches that truth is not reached by mere ritual purity, silence, or intellectual accumulation. A learner encounters the Sikh critique of empty practice and the turn toward lived alignment with truth.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / school curricula / widely explained in introductory Sikh teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਸੋਚੈ ਸੋਚਿ ਨ ਹੋਵਈ ਜੇ ਸੋਚੀ ਲਖ ਵਾਰ ॥
ਚੁਪੈ ਚੁਪ ਨ ਹੋਵਈ ਜੇ ਲਾਇ ਰਹਾ ਲਿਵ ਤਾਰ ॥
ਭੁਖਿਆ ਭੁਖ ਨ ਉਤਰੀ ਜੇ ਬੰਨਾ ਪੁਰੀਆ ਭਾਰ ॥
ਸਹਸ ਸਿਆਣਪਾ ਲਖ ਹੋਹਿ ਤ ਇਕ ਨ ਚਲੈ ਨਾਲਿ ॥
ਕਿਵ ਸਚਿਆਰਾ ਹੋਈਐ ਕਿਵ ਕੂੜੈ ਤੁਟੈ ਪਾਲਿ ॥
ਹੁਕਮਿ ਰਜਾਈ ਚਲਣਾ ਨਾਨਕ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਨਾਲਿ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
sochai soch na hovaee je sochee lakh vaar.
chupai chup na hovaee je laae rahaa liv taar.
bhukhiaa bhukh na utree je bannaa pureeaa bhaar.
sahas siaanapaa lakh hohi ta ik na chalai naal.
kiv sachiaaraa hoeeai kiv koorrai tutai paal.
hukam rajaaee chalnaa naanak likhiaa naal. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: By thinking, He is not obtained, even if one thinks hundreds of thousands of times. By silence, silence is not found, even if one remains absorbed in deep inward stillness; hunger is not appeased by piling up the loads of the world, and countless clevernesses do not go along in the end. How then does one become true, and how is the wall of falsehood broken? Walk in accord with Hukam, O Nanak; this is inscribed along with us.

s1.title: Japji Pauri 2
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮੀ ਹੋਵਨਿ ਆਕਾਰ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਨ ਕਹਿਆ ਜਾਈ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This introduces Hukam, the divine order through which forms arise and life unfolds. A learner gains one of the central Sikh ideas for understanding ego, acceptance, and moral responsibility.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / foundational Sikh philosophy classes / widely quoted in discourse
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਹੁਕਮੀ ਹੋਵਨਿ ਆਕਾਰ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਨ ਕਹਿਆ ਜਾਈ ॥
ਹੁਕਮੀ ਹੋਵਨਿ ਜੀਅ ਹੁਕਮਿ ਮਿਲੈ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥
ਹੁਕਮੀ ਉਤਮੁ ਨੀਚੁ ਹੁਕਮਿ ਲਿਖਿ ਦੁਖ ਸੁਖ ਪਾਈਅਹਿ ॥
ਇਕਨਾ ਹੁਕਮੀ ਬਖਸੀਸ ਇਕਿ ਹੁਕਮੀ ਸਦਾ ਭਵਾਈਅਹਿ ॥
ਹੁਕਮੈ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਸਭੁ ਕੋ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਹੁਕਮ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥
ਨਾਨਕ ਹੁਕਮੈ ਜੇ ਬੁਝੈ ਤ ਹਉਮੈ ਕਹੈ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥੨॥
s1.romanization:
hukmee hovan aakaar hukam na kahi-aa jaaee.
hukmee hovan jeea hukam milai vadi-aaee.
hukmee utam neech hukam likh dukh sukh paaee-ah.
iknaa hukmee bakhsees ik hukmee sadaa bhavaaee-ah.
hukmai andar sabh ko baahar hukam na koe.
naanak hukmai je bujhai ta haumai kahai na koe. ||2||
s1.english_meaning: By Hukam, forms come into being, though Hukam itself cannot be spoken. By Hukam, beings arise; by Hukam, greatness is obtained; by Hukam, some are high and some low, and by what is inscribed under Hukam they receive pain and pleasure. Some, by Hukam, receive grace; some, by Hukam, wander forever. All are within Hukam; none is outside it. O Nanak, one who understands Hukam does not speak in ego.

s1.title: Japji Pauri 8
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਸੁਣਿਐ ਸਿਧ ਪੀਰ ਸੁਰਿ ਨਾਥ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This begins the famous Suniai sequence, showing listening as a transformative spiritual discipline rather than passive hearing. A learner gains the idea that receptivity to divine wisdom changes status, perception, and inner capacity.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / often taught as part of Suniai section / memorization circles
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਸੁਣਿਐ ਸਿਧ ਪੀਰ ਸੁਰਿ ਨਾਥ ॥
ਸੁਣਿਐ ਧਰਤਿ ਧਵਲ ਆਕਾਸ ॥
ਸੁਣਿਐ ਦੀਪ ਲੋਅ ਪਾਤਾਲ ॥
ਸੁਣਿਐ ਪੋਹਿ ਨ ਸਕੈ ਕਾਲੁ ॥
ਨਾਨਕ ਭਗਤਾ ਸਦਾ ਵਿਗਾਸੁ ॥
ਸੁਣਿਐ ਦੂਖ ਪਾਪ ਕਾ ਨਾਸੁ ॥
s1.romanization:
suni-ai sidh peer sur naath.
suni-ai dharat dhaval aakaas.
suni-ai deep lo-a paataal.
suni-ai poh na sakai kaal.
naanak bhagtaa sadaa vigaas.
suni-ai dookh paap kaa naas.
s1.english_meaning: By listening, one is counted among siddhas, pirs, gods, and yogic masters; by listening, the earth, the cosmic bull, and the sky are understood, as are continents, worlds, and nether regions. By listening, death cannot strike; O Nanak, the devotees remain forever in bloom, and by listening, sorrow and sin are destroyed.

s1.title: Japji Closing Salok
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਪਵਣੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਪਾਣੀ ਪਿਤਾ ਮਾਤਾ ਧਰਤਿ ਮਹਤੁ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This frames the natural world as kin and teacher, linking spirituality with humility and ecological awareness. A learner gains one of the most memorable Sikh images for reverence, responsibility, and cosmic belonging.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / environmental Sikh discourse / widely quoted in diaspora
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਪਵਣੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਪਾਣੀ ਪਿਤਾ ਮਾਤਾ ਧਰਤਿ ਮਹਤੁ ॥
ਦਿਵਸੁ ਰਾਤਿ ਦੁਇ ਦਾਈ ਦਾਇਆ ਖੇਲੈ ਸਗਲ ਜਗਤੁ ॥
s1.romanization:
pavan guroo paanee pitaa maataa dharat mahat.
divas raat dui daaee daayaa khelai sagal jagat.
s1.english_meaning: Air is the Guru, water the father, and great earth the mother. Day and night are the two nurses in whose care the whole world plays.

s1.title: Anand Sahib Opening
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਅਨੰਦੁ ਭਇਆ ਮੇਰੀ ਮਾਏ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੂ ਮੈ ਪਾਇਆ
s1.author: Guru Amar Das
s1.why_dense: This announces spiritual joy as the fruit of meeting the True Guru, making bliss a lived state rather than a vague emotion. A learner gains language for Sikh joy that is heard at ceremonies and tied to guidance, not mere festivity.
s1.citation_signal: Anand Karaj ceremony / Ardas conclusion / Nitnem selections
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਅਨੰਦੁ ਭਇਆ ਮੇਰੀ ਮਾਏ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੂ ਮੈ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਤ ਪਾਇਆ ਸਹਜ ਸੇਤੀ ਮਨਿ ਵਜੀਆ ਵਾਧਾਈਆ ॥
ਰਾਗ ਰਤਨ ਪਰਵਾਰ ਪਰੀਆ ਸਬਦ ਗਾਵਣ ਆਈਆ ॥
ਸਬਦੋ ਤ ਗਾਵਹੁ ਹਰੀ ਕੇਰਾ ਮਨਿ ਜਿਨੀ ਵਸਾਇਆ ॥
ਕਹੈ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਅਨੰਦੁ ਹੋਆ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੂ ਮੈ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
anand bhaiaa meree maae satiguroo mai paaiaa.
satiguru ta paaiaa sahaj setee man vajeeaa vaadhaaeeaa.
raag ratan parvaar pareeaa sabad gaavan aaeeaa.
sabado ta gaavahu haree keraa man jinee vasaaiaa.
kahai naanak anand hoaa satiguroo mai paaiaa. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: Bliss has arisen, O my mother, for I have found the True Guru. Finding the True Guru in equipoise, the mind resounds with songs of rejoicing; the jewel-like melodies and their families come as celestial maidens to sing the Shabad. Sing only the Word of the Lord, O those in whose minds He dwells; says Nanak, bliss has come, for I have found the True Guru.

s1.title: Anand Sahib Pauri on Listening
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਏ ਮਨ ਮੇਰਿਆ ਤੂ ਸਦਾ ਰਹੁ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਲੇ
s1.author: Guru Amar Das
s1.why_dense: This addresses the mind directly and teaches constant companionship with the Divine through attentive listening to the Guru's word. A learner gains a practical inner instruction that connects emotion, memory, and discipline.
s1.citation_signal: Anand Sahib recitation / ceremonies / kirtan programs
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਏ ਮਨ ਮੇਰਿਆ ਤੂ ਸਦਾ ਰਹੁ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਲੇ ॥
ਹਰਿ ਨਾਲਿ ਰਹੁ ਤੂ ਮੰਨ ਮੇਰੇ ਦੂਖ ਸਭਿ ਵਿਸਾਰਣਾ ॥
ਅੰਗੀਕਾਰੁ ਓਹੁ ਕਰੇ ਤੇਰਾ ਕਾਰਜ ਸਭਿ ਸਵਾਰਣਾ ॥
ਸਭਨਾ ਗਲਾ ਸਮਰਥੁ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਸੋ ਕਿਉ ਮਨਹੁ ਵਿਸਾਰੇ ॥
ਕਹੈ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਮੰਨ ਮੇਰੇ ਸਦਾ ਰਹੁ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਲੇ ॥
ਏ ਮਨ ਮੇਰਿਆ ਤੂ ਸਦਾ ਰਹੁ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਲੇ ॥
s1.romanization:
e man meriaa too sadaa rahu har naale.
har naal rahu too mann mere dookh sabh visaarnaa.
angeekaar oh kare teraa kaaraj sabh savaarnaa.
sabhnaa galaa samrath suaamee so kio manhu visaare.
kahai naanak mann mere sadaa rahu har naale.
e man meriaa too sadaa rahu har naale.
s1.english_meaning: O my mind, remain always with the Lord. Staying with the Lord, all sorrows are forgotten; He takes you into His care and sets all your affairs in order. The Master is capable in all matters—why should He be forgotten from the mind? Says Nanak: O my mind, remain forever with the Lord.

s1.title: Sukhmani Mangalacharan
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਆਦਿ ਗੁਰਏ ਨਮਹ ਜੁਗਾਦਿ ਗੁਰਏ ਨਮਹ
s1.author: Guru Arjan Dev
s1.why_dense: This invocation honors the Guru as timeless across ages, making reverence itself a theological statement. A learner gains a compact liturgical formula often used independently as a blessing and entry into Sukhmani's contemplative world.
s1.citation_signal: Sukhmani Sahib recitation / household prayer / widely quoted in diaspora
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
ਆਦਿ ਗੁਰਏ ਨਮਹ ॥
ਜੁਗਾਦਿ ਗੁਰਏ ਨਮਹ ॥
ਸਤਿਗੁਰਏ ਨਮਹ ॥
ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰਦੇਵਏ ਨਮਹ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
ik-oankar satigur prasaad.
aad gure namah.
jugaad gure namah.
satigure namah.
shree gurdeve namah. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: One Universal Creator; by the grace of the True Guru. Salutation to the Guru in the beginning, salutation to the Guru through the ages, salutation to the True Guru, salutation to the revered Divine Guru. The invocation bows to the Guru as primal, eternal, true, and radiant.

s1.title: Sukhmani Ashtapadi 1 Opening
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਸਿਮਰਉ ਸਿਮਰਿ ਸਿਮਰਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਵਉ
s1.author: Guru Arjan Dev
s1.why_dense: This teaches remembrance of the Divine as the path to peace, making simran both method and result. A learner gains a highly repeatable phrase that introduces one of the most central Sikh practices in accessible language.
s1.citation_signal: Sukhmani Sahib / simran gatherings / school and camp teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਸਲੋਕੁ ॥
ਆਦਿ ਗੁਰਏ ਨਮਹ ॥ ਜੁਗਾਦਿ ਗੁਰਏ ਨਮਹ ॥
ਸਤਿਗੁਰਏ ਨਮਹ ॥ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰਦੇਵਏ ਨਮਹ ॥੧॥
ਸਿਮਰਉ ਸਿਮਰਿ ਸਿਮਰਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਵਉ ॥ ਕਲਿ ਕਲੇਸ ਤਨ ਮਾਹਿ ਮਿਟਾਵਉ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
salok.
aad gure namah. jugaad gure namah.
satigure namah. sree gurdeve namah.1.
simrau simar simar sukh paavau. kal kales tan maahi mitaavau.1.
s1.english_meaning: Salutation to the Primal Guru, salutation to the Guru through the ages; salutation to the True Guru, salutation to the Divine Guru. Meditating, remembering, and again remembering, I obtain peace; the strife and suffering within the body are erased.

s1.title: Sukhmani on the Human Birth
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਭਈ ਪਰਾਪਤਿ ਮਾਨੁਖ ਦੇਹੁਰੀਆ
s1.author: Guru Arjan Dev
s1.why_dense: This teaches that human life is rare and should be used for union with the Divine rather than wasted in distraction. A learner gains a moral urgency that is frequently cited in sermons, camps, and family instruction.
s1.citation_signal: widely quoted in katha / school curricula / diaspora teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਭਈ ਪਰਾਪਤਿ ਮਾਨੁਖ ਦੇਹੁਰੀਆ ॥
ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਮਿਲਣ ਕੀ ਇਹ ਤੇਰੀ ਬਰੀਆ ॥
ਅਵਰਿ ਕਾਜ ਤੇਰੈ ਕਿਤੈ ਨ ਕਾਮ ॥
ਮਿਲੁ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਭਜੁ ਕੇਵਲ ਨਾਮ ॥
s1.romanization:
bhaee paraapat maanukh dehureeaa.
gobind milan kee ih teree bareeaa.
avar kaaj terai kitai na kaam.
mil saadhsangat bhaj keval naam.
s1.english_meaning: This human body has been obtained; this is your turn to meet the Lord of the Universe. Other tasks will be of no use to you; join the company of the holy and meditate only on the Naam.

s1.title: Sukhmani on the Brahm Gyani
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗਿਆਨੀ ਸਦਾ ਨਿਰਲੇਪ
s1.author: Guru Arjan Dev
s1.why_dense: This defines the realized person through detachment, steadiness, and spiritual insight rather than social status. A learner gains a portrait of Sikh sainthood that sharpens ethical aspiration and interpretive vocabulary.
s1.citation_signal: Sukhmani Sahib / academic citation / katha on saintliness
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਸਲੋਕੁ ॥
ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗਿਆਨੀ ਸਦਾ ਨਿਰਲੇਪ ॥ ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲ ਮਹਿ ਕਮਲ ਅਲੇਪ ॥
ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗਿਆਨੀ ਸਦਾ ਨਿਰਦੋਖ ॥ ਜੈਸੇ ਸੂਰੁ ਸਰਬ ਕਉ ਸੋਖ ॥੧॥
ਅਸਟਪਦੀ ॥
ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗਿਆਨੀ ਕੈ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਸਮਾਨਿ ॥ ਜੈਸੇ ਰਾਜ ਰੰਕ ਕਉ ਲਾਗੈ ਤੁਲਿ ਪਵਾਨੁ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
salok.
braham giaanee sadaa nirlep. jaise jal meh kamal alep.
braham giaanee sadaa nirdokh. jaise soor sarab kau sokh.1.
astapadee.
braham giaanee kai drisat samaan. jaise raaj rank kau laagai tul pavaan.1.
s1.english_meaning: The knower of Brahman remains forever unstained, as the lotus stays untouched within the water. The knower of Brahman is ever without fault, as the sun draws moisture from all alike. In the vision of the knower of Brahman, all are equal, just as the wind touches king and beggar in the same measure.

s1.title: So Dar
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਦਰੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਕੇਹਾ ਸੋ ਘਰੁ ਕੇਹਾ ਜਿਤੁ ਬਹਿ ਸਰਬ ਸਮਾਲੇ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This imagines the divine court through music, order, and wonder, expanding devotion into cosmic vision. A learner gains rich imagery and liturgical familiarity because it is heard regularly in evening prayer.
s1.citation_signal: Rehras Sahib / evening Nitnem / Gurudwara recitation
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਸੋ ਦਰੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਕੇਹਾ ਸੋ ਘਰੁ ਕੇਹਾ ਜਿਤੁ ਬਹਿ ਸਰਬ ਸਮਾਲੇ ॥
ਵਾਜੇ ਤੇਰੇ ਨਾਦ ਅਨੇਕ ਅਸੰਖਾ ਕੇਤੇ ਤੇਰੇ ਵਾਵਣਹਾਰੇ ॥
ਕੇਤੇ ਤੇਰੇ ਰਾਗ ਪਰੀ ਸਿਉ ਕਹੀਅਹਿ ਕੇਤੇ ਤੇਰੇ ਗਾਵਣਹਾਰੇ ॥
ਗਾਵਨਿ ਤੁਧਨੋ ਪਵਣੁ ਪਾਣੀ ਬੈਸੰਤਰੁ ਗਾਵੈ ਰਾਜਾ ਧਰਮੁ ਦੁਆਰੇ ॥
s1.romanization:
so dar teraa keha so ghar keha jit bahi sarab samaale.
vaaje tere naad anek asankhaa kete tere vaavanhaare.
kete tere raag paree sio kaheeahi kete tere gaavanhaare.
gaavan tudhno pavan paanee baisantar gaavai raajaa dharam duaare.
s1.english_meaning: What is that Door of Yours, what is that House, seated wherein You care for all? Countless are Your sounds and instruments, countless those who play and sing; the airs, the waters, and the fire sing to You, and Dharam Raja sings at Your gate.

s1.title: Dhan Dhan Ram Das Gur
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਧੰਨੁ ਧੰਨੁ ਰਾਮਦਾਸ ਗੁਰੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਸਿਰਿਆ ਤਿਨੈ ਸਵਾਰਿਆ
s1.author: Guru Arjan Dev
s1.why_dense: This praises Guru Ram Das as the one who refines and uplifts human life through grace. A learner gains a culturally ubiquitous congregational hymn that teaches gratitude, lineage, and the role of the Guru.
s1.citation_signal: widely sung in Gurudwaras / Gurpurab programs / diaspora kirtan
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਧੰਨੁ ਧੰਨੁ ਰਾਮਦਾਸ ਗੁਰੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਸਿਰਿਆ ਤਿਨੈ ਸਵਾਰਿਆ ॥
ਪੂਰੀ ਹੋਈ ਕਰਾਮਾਤਿ ਆਪਿ ਸਿਰਜਣਹਾਰੈ ਧਾਰਿਆ ॥
ਸਿਖੀ ਅਤੈ ਸੰਗਤੀ ਪਾਰਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਕਰਿ ਨਮਸਕਾਰਿਆ ॥
ਅਟਲੁ ਅਥਾਹੁ ਅਤੋਲੁ ਤੂ ਤੇਰਾ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਪਾਰਾਵਾਰਿਆ ॥
s1.romanization:
dhann dhann raamdaas gur jin siriyaa tinai savaariyaa.
pooree hoee karaamaat aap sirjanhaarai dhaariyaa.
sikhee atai sangatee paarbrahm kar namaskaariyaa.
atal athaah atol too teraa ant na paaraavaariyaa.
s1.english_meaning: Blessed, blessed is Guru Ram Das; the One who created him has adorned him. The wondrous act has been fulfilled by the Creator Himself; Sikhi and the holy congregation bow as to the Supreme Lord. You are immovable, unfathomable, and beyond measure; Your limit and far shore cannot be found.

s1.title: Tu Thakur Tum Pe Ardas
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਠਾਕੁਰੁ ਤੁਮ ਪਹਿ ਅਰਦਾਸਿ
s1.author: Guru Arjan Dev
s1.why_dense: This is a direct prayer of dependence, humility, and surrender before the Divine. A learner gains the emotional grammar of Sikh supplication in language simple enough to internalize and reuse.
s1.citation_signal: widely quoted in Ardas-related teaching / kirtan / household prayer
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਤੂ ਠਾਕੁਰੁ ਤੁਮ ਪਹਿ ਅਰਦਾਸਿ ॥
ਜੀਉ ਪਿੰਡੁ ਸਭੁ ਤੇਰੀ ਰਾਸਿ ॥
ਤੁਮ ਮਾਤ ਪਿਤਾ ਹਮ ਬਾਰਿਕ ਤੇਰੇ ॥
ਤੁਮਰੀ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਮਹਿ ਸੂਖ ਘਨੇਰੇ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
Too thaakur tum peh ardaas.
Jeeo pind sabh teree raas.
Tum maat pitaa ham baarik tere.
Tumree kirpaa meh sookh ghanere. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: You are the Master; to You I offer my prayer. My soul and body are all Your capital. You are my mother and father; I am Your child. Within Your grace are abundant comforts and deep peace.

s1.title: Tera Kiya Meetha Lage
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਤੇਰਾ ਕੀਆ ਮੀਠਾ ਲਾਗੈ
s1.author: Guru Arjan Dev
s1.why_dense: This teaches radical acceptance of divine will even amid suffering, making sweetness a spiritual discipline rather than a mood. A learner gains a concise expression of resilience that is often invoked in grief and hardship.
s1.citation_signal: widely quoted in sermons / bereavement contexts / diaspora familiarity
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਤੇਰਾ ਕੀਆ ਮੀਠਾ ਲਾਗੈ ॥
ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਪਦਾਰਥੁ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਮਾਂਗੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
ਸਿਮਰਉ ਜਾਸੁ ਬਿਸੁੰਭਰ ਏਕੈ ॥
ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਤ ਅਗਨਤ ਅਨੇਕੈ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
Teraa keeaa meethaa laagai.
Har naam padaarath Naanak maangai. ||1|| Rahaao.
Simarau jaas bisumbhar ekai.
Naam japat aganat anekai. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: Sweet seems to me Your doing; Nanak asks only for the treasure of the Lord’s Name. I remember the One Sustainer of the world; by meditating on the Name, countless multitudes have been carried through.

s1.title: Deh Shiva Bar Mohe
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਦੇਹਿ ਸਿਵਾ ਬਰੁ ਮੋਹਿ ਇਹੈ
s1.author: Guru Gobind Singh
s1.why_dense: This asks for the courage to act righteously and never shrink from just struggle. A learner gains a compact statement of Sikh moral bravery that bridges spirituality, discipline, and public action.
s1.citation_signal: school curricula / martial and youth programs / widely quoted in diaspora
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਦੇਹਿ ਸਿਵਾ ਬਰੁ ਮੋਹਿ ਇਹੈ ਸੁਭ ਕਰਮਨ ਤੇ ਕਬਹੂੰ ਨ ਟਰੋਂ ॥
ਨ ਡਰੋਂ ਅਰਿ ਸੋਂ ਜਬ ਜਾਇ ਲਰੋਂ ਨਿਸਚੈ ਕਰਿ ਅਪੁਨੀ ਜੀਤ ਕਰੋਂ ॥
ਅਰੁ ਸਿਖ ਹੋਂ ਆਪਣੇ ਹੀ ਮਨ ਕਉ ਇਹ ਲਾਲਚ ਹਉ ਗੁਨ ਤਉ ਉਚਰੋਂ ॥
ਜਬ ਆਵ ਕੀ ਅਉਧ ਨਿਦਾਨ ਬਨੈ ਅਤਿ ਹੀ ਰਨ ਮੈ ਤਬ ਜੂਝ ਮਰੋਂ ॥੨੩੧॥
s1.romanization:
deh sivaa bar mohe ihai subh karman te kabhoon na taron.
na daron ar so jab jaae laron nischai kar apunee jeet karon.
ar sikh hon apne hee man kau ih laalach hau gun tau ucharon.
jab aav kee audh nidaan banai at hee ran mai tab joojh maron. ||231||
s1.english_meaning: Grant me this boon, O Shiva: may I never shrink from righteous deeds. May I not fear the foe when I go to battle, and with firm resolve may I secure victory; may I instruct my own mind only in this longing, to utter Your virtues. And when the final moment of life arrives, may I fall fighting in the thick of the field.

s1.title: Jaap Sahib Opening
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਚਕ੍ਰ ਚਿਹਨ ਅਰੁ ਬਰਨ ਜਾਤਿ ਅਰੁ ਪਾਤਿ ਨਹਿਨ ਜਿਹ
s1.author: Guru Gobind Singh
s1.why_dense: This declares the Divine beyond marks, caste, lineage, and fixed description, while using dazzling multilingual poetics. A learner gains both theological breadth and exposure to the linguistic range that makes Jaap Sahib culturally distinctive.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / Amrit Sanchar / advanced Punjabi and Gurbani study
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਚਕ੍ਰ ਚਿਹਨ ਅਰੁ ਬਰਨ ਜਾਤਿ ਅਰੁ ਪਾਤਿ ਨਹਿਨ ਜਿਹ ॥
ਰੂਪ ਰੰਗ ਅਰੁ ਰੇਖ ਭੇਖ ਕੋਊ ਕਹਿ ਨ ਸਕਤਿ ਕਿਹ ॥
ਅਚਲ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਅਨਭਉ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਸ ਅਮਿਤੋਜਿ ਕਹਿਜੈ ॥
ਕੋਟਿ ਇੰਦ੍ਰ ਇੰਦ੍ਰਾਣਿ ਸਾਹਿ ਸਾਹਾਣਿ ਗਣਿਜੈ ॥
ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਣ ਮਹੀਪ ਸੁਰ ਨਰ ਅਸੁਰ ਨੇਤ ਨੇਤ ਬਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਣ ਕਹਤ ॥
ਤਵ ਸਰਬ ਨਾਮ ਕਥੈ ਕਵਨ ਕਰਮ ਨਾਮ ਬਰਨਤ ਸੁਮਤਿ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
Chakr chihan ar barn jaat ar paat nahin jih.
Roop rang ar rekh bhekh kooo kahi na sakat kih.
Achal moorat anbhau prakaas amitoj kahijai.
Kot indr indraani saah saahaani ganijai.
Tribhaavan maheep sur nar asur net net ban trin kahat.
Tav sarab naam kathai kavan karam naam baranat sumat. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: He bears no mark or emblem, no color, caste, lineage, or social rank. No one can speak His form, hue, outline, or garb; He is the unmoving image, self-radiant in fearless light, of immeasurable splendor. Countless Indras and their queens, kings and great rulers are reckoned before Him, while sovereigns of the three worlds, gods, humans, and demons say only “not this, not this,” deeming themselves as blades of grass; who can narrate all Your names, and by what discerning wisdom can Your action-names be described?

s1.title: Jaap Sahib Nameless One
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਨਮਸਤ੍ਵੰ ਅਕਾਲੇ ਨਮਸਤ੍ਵੰ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਲੇ
s1.author: Guru Gobind Singh
s1.why_dense: This offers praise through a cascade of divine attributes, balancing transcendence with compassion. A learner gains repeated adjective patterns that build vocabulary while showing how praise can be both poetic and doctrinal.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / Amrit Sanchar / memorization practice
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਨਮਸਤ੍ਵੰ ਅਕਾਲੇ ਨਮਸਤ੍ਵੰ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਲੇ ॥
ਨਮਸਤ੍ਵੰ ਅਰੂਪੇ ਨਮਸਤ੍ਵੰ ਅਨੂਪੇ ॥
ਨਮਸਤ੍ਵੰ ਅਭੇਖੇ ਨਮਸਤ੍ਵੰ ਅਲੇਖੇ ॥
ਨਮਸਤ੍ਵੰ ਅਕਾਏ ਨਮਸਤ੍ਵੰ ਅਜਾਏ ॥
s1.romanization:
namastvang akaale namastvang kripaale.
namastvang aroope namastvang anoope.
namastvang abhekhe namastvang alekhe.
namastvang akaae namastvang ajaae.
s1.english_meaning: Salutation to You, O Timeless One; salutation to You, O Compassionate One. Salutation to You, O Formless One, O Incomparable One; salutation to You, O Garbless and Unwritten One; salutation to You, O Bodiless One, O Unborn One.

s1.title: Tav Prasad Savaiye Opening
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਸ੍ਰਾਵਗ ਸੁਧ ਸਮੂਹ ਸਿਧਾਨ ਕੇ ਦੇਖਿ ਫਿਰਿਓ ਘਰਿ ਜੋਗਿ ਜਤੀ ਕੇ
s1.ang_or_location: Akal Ustat / Tav Prasad Savaiye section
s1.author: Guru Gobind Singh
s1.why_dense: This surveys religious types and disciplines only to expose the insufficiency of outward identity without inner realization. A learner gains a sharp Sikh critique of ritualism in a memorable, rhetorically forceful form.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / Amrit Sanchar / frequently taught in Sikh studies
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਸ੍ਰਾਵਗ ਸੁਧ ਸਮੂਹ ਸਿਧਾਨ ਕੇ ਦੇਖਿ ਫਿਰਿਓ ਘਰਿ ਜੋਗਿ ਜਤੀ ਕੇ ॥
ਸੂਰ ਸੁਰਾਰਦਨ ਸੁਧ ਸੁਧਾਦਿਕ ਸੰਤ ਸਮੂਹ ਅਨੇਕ ਮਤੀ ਕੇ ॥
ਸਾਰੇ ਹੀ ਦੇਸ ਕੋ ਦੇਖਿ ਰਹਿਓ ਮਤ ਕੋਊ ਨ ਦੇਖੀਅਤ ਪ੍ਰਾਨਪਤੀ ਕੇ ॥
ਸ੍ਰੀ ਭਗਵਾਨ ਕੀ ਭਾਇ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਹੂੰ ਤੇ ਏਕ ਰਤੀ ਬਿਨੁ ਏਕ ਰਤੀ ਕੇ ॥
s1.romanization:
sraavag sudh samooh sidhaan ke dekhi phirio ghar jog jatee ke.
soor suraardan sudh sudhaadik sant samooh anek matee ke.
saare hee des ko dekhi rahio mat kooo na dekheeat praanpatee ke.
sree bhagvaan kee bhaai kirpaa hoon te ek ratee bin ek ratee ke.
s1.english_meaning: I have looked among the pure Shravaks, the assemblies of Siddhas, and in the houses of yogis and celibate ascetics. I have seen hosts of heroes, gods, demons, refined beings, and many-minded saints throughout all lands, yet I beheld none equal to the Lord of life; only by the gracious favor of the Blessed Lord does one receive even a particle of that unique essence.

s1.title: Jin Prem Kiyo
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਕੀਓ ਤਿਨ ਹੀ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਪਾਇਓ
s1.author: Guru Gobind Singh
s1.why_dense: This teaches that divine realization comes through love, not display, argument, or status. A learner gains one of the most quoted Sikh lines and a simple criterion for authentic spirituality.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / widely quoted in diaspora / youth and camp teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਕਾਹੂ ਲੈ ਪਾਹਨ ਪੂਜ ਧਰਿਓ ਸਿਰ ਕਾਹੂ ਲੈ ਲਿੰਗੁ ਗਰੇ ਲਟਕਾਇਓ ॥
ਕਾਹੂ ਲਖਿਓ ਹਰਿ ਅਵਾਚੀ ਦਿਸਾ ਮਹਿ ਕਾਹੂ ਪਛਾਹ ਕੋ ਸੀਸੁ ਨਿਵਾਇਓ ॥
ਕੋਊ ਬੁਤਾਨ ਕੋ ਪੂਜਤ ਹੈ ਪਸੁ ਕੋਊ ਮ੍ਰਿਤਾਨ ਕੋ ਪੂਜਨ ਧਾਇਓ ॥
ਕੂਰ ਕ੍ਰਿਆ ਉਰਝਿਓ ਸਭ ਹੀ ਜਗੁ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਭਗਵਾਨ ਕੋ ਭੇਦੁ ਨ ਪਾਇਓ ॥੮੬॥
s1.romanization:
Kaahoo lai paahan pooj dhario sir kaahoo lai ling gare latkaaio.
Kaahoo lakhio har avaachee disaa meh kaahoo pachhaah ko sees nivaaio.
Kooo butaan ko poojat hai pasu kooo mritaan ko poojan dhaaio.
Koor kiriaa urjhio sabh hee jag sree bhagvaan ko bhed na paaio. ||86||
s1.english_meaning: Some take up stone and worship it upon their head; some hang the emblem at their neck. Some behold the Divine in the unseen direction, and some bow their heads toward the west. Some worship idols, some hasten to adore the dead; the whole world is entangled in false rites and has not obtained the mystery of the Blessed Lord.

s1.title: Chaupai Sahib Opening
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਹਮਰੀ ਕਰੋ ਹਾਥ ਦੈ ਰਛਾ
s1.author: Guru Gobind Singh
s1.why_dense: This is a direct plea for protection, guidance, and preservation from harm and inner weakness. A learner gains a widely recited prayer form that introduces the protective register of Sikh devotion.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / Rehras Sahib / bedtime and travel recitation
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਹਮਰੀ ਕਰੋ ਹਾਥ ਦੈ ਰਛਾ ॥
ਪੂਰਨ ਹੋਇ ਚਿਤ ਕੀ ਇਛਾ ॥
ਤਵ ਚਰਨਨ ਮਨ ਰਹੈ ਹਮਾਰਾ ॥
ਅਪਨਾ ਜਾਨ ਕਰੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਰਾ ॥
s1.romanization:
hamree karo haath dai rachhaa ||
pooran hoi chit kee ichhaa ||
tav charanan man rahai hamaaraa ||
apnaa jaan karo pratipaaraa ||
s1.english_meaning: Protect me by Thy hand; let the desire of the mind be fulfilled. May my mind remain at Thy feet; knowing me as Thine own, sustain and cherish me.

s1.title: Chaupai Sahib Refuge Line
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਰਾਖ ਲੇਹੁ ਮੋਹਿ ਰਾਖਨਹਾਰੇ
s1.author: Guru Gobind Singh
s1.why_dense: This condenses vulnerability and trust into a single cry for refuge before the Divine Protector. A learner gains emotionally immediate language that is easy to remember and commonly used in personal prayer.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / household prayer / widely quoted in difficult times
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਰਾਖ ਲੇਹੁ ਮੋਹਿ ਰਾਖਨਹਾਰੇ ॥
ਸਾਹਿਬ ਸੰਤ ਸਹਾਇ ਪਿਆਰੇ ॥
ਦੀਨਬੰਧੁ ਦੁਸਟਨ ਕੇ ਹੰਤਾ ॥
ਤੁਮ ਹੋ ਪੁਰੀ ਚਤੁਰਦਸ ਕੰਤਾ ॥
s1.romanization:
Raakh lehu mohi raakhanhaare.
Saahib sant sahaa-e piaare.
Deenbandh dustan ke hanta.
Tum ho puree chaturdas kanta.
s1.english_meaning: Protect me, O Protector; O Master, beloved helper of the saints. O friend of the lowly, destroyer of the wicked, You are the Lord of all the fourteen realms.

s1.title: Salok Mahalla 9 on Fearlessness
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਭੈ ਕਾਹੂ ਕਉ ਦੇਤ ਨਹਿ ਨਹਿ ਭੈ ਮਾਨਤ ਆਨ
s1.author: Guru Tegh Bahadur
s1.why_dense: This defines the ideal person as one who neither intimidates others nor lives in fear of them. A learner gains a concise Sikh ethic of dignity, courage, and non-domination that applies immediately to daily life.
s1.citation_signal: widely quoted in ethical discourse / school curricula / diaspora teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਭੈ ਕਾਹੂ ਕਉ ਦੇਤ ਨਹਿ ਨਹਿ ਭੈ ਮਾਨਤ ਆਨਿ ॥
ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਸੁਨਿ ਰੇ ਮਨਾ ਗਿਆਨੀ ਤਾਹਿ ਬਖਾਨਿ ॥
s1.romanization:
bhai kaahoo kau det neh nehi bhai maanat aan.
kaho naanak sun re manaa giaanee taahi bakhaan.
s1.english_meaning: One who gives fear to none, and who accepts no fear from another—O Nanak, listen, O mind: call that one truly wise. Fearlessness here is joined with harmlessness; such a being is known as the knower.

s1.title: Salok Mahalla 9 on Impermanence
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਨਰੁ ਦੁਖ ਮੈ ਦੁਖੁ ਨਹੀ ਮਾਨੈ
s1.author: Guru Tegh Bahadur
s1.why_dense: This teaches equanimity amid pain, pleasure, praise, and blame, outlining inner freedom in practical terms. A learner gains a disciplined model of steadiness that resonates with both spiritual and psychological growth.
s1.citation_signal: widely quoted in katha / school and camp teaching / diaspora familiarity
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਜੋ ਨਰੁ ਦੁਖ ਮੈ ਦੁਖੁ ਨਹੀ ਮਾਨੈ ॥
ਸੁਖ ਸਨੇਹੁ ਅਰੁ ਭੈ ਨਹੀ ਜਾ ਕੈ ਕਾਂਚਨ ਮਾਟੀ ਮਾਨੈ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
jo nar dukh mai dukh nahee maanai.
sukh sanehu ar bhai nahee jaa kai kaanchan maatee maanai. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: That person who, in suffering, does not accept suffering as affliction, and who is untouched by attachment to pleasure or by fear, regards gold and dust alike. Such a one stands beyond the sway of worldly opposites.

s1.title: Farida on Longing
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਬਿਰਹਾ ਬਿਰਹਾ ਆਖੀਐ ਬਿਰਹਾ ਤੂ ਸੁਲਤਾਨੁ
s1.author: Baba Farid
s1.why_dense: This elevates longing into a sovereign spiritual force, turning absence into a path of awakening. A learner gains a bridge between Punjabi poetic culture and Gurbani's interior language of love and separation.
s1.citation_signal: widely cited in Punjabi literary culture / folk and modern music / classroom discussion
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਬਿਰਹਾ ਬਿਰਹਾ ਆਖੀਐ ਬਿਰਹਾ ਤੂ ਸੁਲਤਾਨੁ ॥
ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜਿਤੁ ਤਨਿ ਬਿਰਹੁ ਨ ਊਪਜੈ ਸੋ ਤਨੁ ਜਾਣੁ ਮਸਾਨੁ ॥
s1.romanization:
birhaa birhaa aakheeai birhaa too sultaan.
fareedaa jit tan birahu na oopjai so tan jaan masaan.
s1.english_meaning: They call it longing, longing; longing, you are the sovereign. Farid, know that body in which longing does not arise to be a cremation ground.

s1.title: Farida on Outer and Inner Darkness
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕਾਲੇ ਮੈਡੇ ਕਪੜੇ ਕਾਲਾ ਮੈਡਾ ਵੇਸੁ
s1.author: Baba Farid
s1.why_dense: This contrasts visible appearance with the deeper stain of one's actions and inner state. A learner gains accessible classical Punjabi diction and a sharp moral lesson against hypocrisy.
s1.citation_signal: widely quoted in Punjabi culture / school curricula / literary anthologies
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕਾਲੇ ਮੈਡੇ ਕਪੜੇ ਕਾਲਾ ਮੈਡਾ ਵੇਸੁ ॥
ਗੁਨਹੀ ਭਰਿਆ ਮੈ ਫਿਰਾ ਲੋਕੁ ਕਹੈ ਦਰਵੇਸੁ ॥੧੯॥
s1.romanization:
Fareedaa kaale maide kaprhe kaalaa maidaa ves.
Gunhee bhariaa mai phiraa lok kahai darves. ||19||
s1.english_meaning: Farid says: my clothes are black, and black is my attire. Filled with sins I wander, yet the people call me a dervish.

s1.title: Farida on the Beautiful Season
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਰੁਤੀ ਰੁਤਿ ਸੁਹਾਵੜੀ ਜਿਤੁ ਤੂੰ ਸਜਣੁ ਮਿਲਿਆ
s1.author: Baba Farid
s1.why_dense: This ties seasonality to spiritual meeting, making outer climate a metaphor for inner fulfillment. A learner gains rare seasonal imagery in canonical scripture and a strong connection to Punjabi sensibility.
s1.citation_signal: Punjabi literary study / seasonal and folk resonance / cited in cultural teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਫਰੀਦਾ ਰੁਤੀ ਰੁਤਿ ਸੁਹਾਵੜੀ ਜਿਤੁ ਤੂੰ ਸਜਣੁ ਮਿਲਿਆ ॥
ਤੈ ਜੇਵਡੁ ਮੈ ਨਾਹਿ ਕੋ ਸਭੁ ਜਗੁ ਡਿਠਾ ਹੰਢਿ ॥੧੦੦॥
s1.romanization:
Fareedaa rutee rut suhaavarhee jit toon sajan mili-aa.
Tai jevad mai naahi ko sabh jag dithaa handh.
s1.english_meaning: Farid says: beautiful is that season, that time, in which I met You, O Beloved. Roaming and seeing the whole world, I found none equal to You.

s1.title: Kabir on the Fragile Body
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਤਨੁ ਕਾਚਾ ਕੁੰਭੁ ਹੈ
s1.author: Kabir
s1.why_dense: This compares the body to an unbaked clay pot, stressing fragility and the urgency of spiritual awareness. A learner gains a vivid metaphor that opens discussion of mortality in simple, memorable Punjabi-Hindi diction.
s1.citation_signal: widely cited in katha / interfaith Gurbani teaching / classroom use
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਇਹੁ ਤਨੁ ਕਾਚਾ ਕੁੰਭੁ ਹੈ ਲੀਆ ਫਿਰੈ ਥਾ ਸਾਥਿ ॥
ਢਬਕਾ ਲਗਾ ਫੂਟਿ ਗਇਆ ਕਛੂ ਨ ਆਇਆ ਹਾਥਿ ॥੧॥
ਇਹੁ ਤਨੁ ਐਸਾ ਜੈਸੇ ਘਾਸ ਕੀ ਟਾਟੀ ॥
ਜਲਿ ਗਇਓ ਘਾਸੁ ਰਲਿ ਗਇਓ ਮਾਟੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
s1.romanization:
ih tan kaachaa kumbh hai leeaa firai thaa saath.
dhabkaa lagaa phoot gi-aa kachhoo na aa-i-aa haath. ||1||
ih tan aisaa jaise ghaas kee taatee.
jal ga-i-o ghaas ral ga-i-o maatee. ||1|| rahaao ||
s1.english_meaning: This body is an unbaked clay pitcher, carried about as a companion; when it receives a blow, it shatters, and nothing comes into the hand. This body is like a fence of dry grass: when the grass is burned, it mingles back into dust.

s1.title: Nanak Naam Chardi Kala
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮ ਚੜ੍ਹਦੀ ਕਲਾ ਤੇਰੇ ਭਾਣੇ ਸਰਬੱਤ ਦਾ ਭਲਾ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This links remembrance of the Divine with resilient spirit and the welfare of all, making spirituality inseparable from collective good. A learner gains one of the most culturally recognizable Sikh formulations of optimism and universal concern.
s1.citation_signal: Ardas / universally heard in Gurudwaras / widely quoted in diaspora
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮ ਚੜ੍ਹਦੀ ਕਲਾ ।
ਤੇਰੇ ਭਾਣੇ ਸਰਬਤ ਦਾ ਭਲਾ ॥
s1.romanization:
Naanak Naam charhdee kalaa.
Tere bhaane sarabat daa bhalaa.
s1.english_meaning: O Nanak, through the Naam there is ever-rising spirit. In Your Will, may there be welfare for all.

s1.title: Waheguru Gurmantra Simran
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਵਾਹਿਗੁਰੂ
s1.author: Gurmat tradition
s1.why_dense: This single utterance condenses wonder, praise, and remembrance into the most accessible form of Sikh devotion. A learner gains the sound-symbol most likely heard at home, in sangat, and in meditative repetition across generations.
s1.citation_signal: simran gatherings / household practice / universally heard in diaspora
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi: ਵਾਹਿਗੁਰੂ
s1.romanization: Vaahiguroo
s1.english_meaning: Vaahiguroo is the wondrous Enlightener, invoked in Sikh devotional practice as the Gurmantra. It is revered in Gurmat tradition as the utterance that turns consciousness toward the Divine.

s1.title: Japji Pauri 4 — Amrit Velaa
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਸਾਚਾ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਸਾਚੁ ਨਾਇ ਭਾਖਿਆ ਭਾਉ ਅਪਾਰੁ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This pauri introduces Amrit Velaa — the pre-dawn hours — as the optimal time for meditation on the True Name, and teaches that merit comes through grace (nadar), not effort alone. A learner gains one of the most practically actionable instructions in Gurbani: wake early, remember the Name.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / Gurudwara daily routine / taught in all introductory Sikh education
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਸਾਚਾ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਸਾਚੁ ਨਾਇ ਭਾਖਿਆ ਭਾਉ ਅਪਾਰੁ ॥
ਆਖਹਿ ਮੰਗਹਿ ਦੇਹਿ ਦੇਹਿ ਦਾਤਿ ਕਰੇ ਦਾਤਾਰੁ ॥
ਫੇਰਿ ਕਿ ਅਗੈ ਰਖੀਐ ਜਿਤੁ ਦਿਸੈ ਦਰਬਾਰੁ ॥
ਮੁਹੌ ਕਿ ਬੋਲਣੁ ਬੋਲੀਐ ਜਿਤੁ ਸੁਣਿ ਧਰੇ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥
ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਵੇਲਾ ਸਚੁ ਨਾਉ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
ਕਰਮੀ ਆਵੈ ਕਪੜਾ ਨਦਰੀ ਮੋਖੁ ਦੁਆਰੁ ॥
ਨਾਨਕ ਏਵੈ ਜਾਣੀਐ ਸਭੁ ਆਪੇ ਸਚਿਆਰੁ ॥੪॥
s1.romanization:
saachaa saahib saach naae bhaakhiaa bhaau apaar.
aakheh mangeh deh deh daat kare daataar.
fer ki agai rakheeai jit disai darbaar.
muhou ki bolan boleeai jit sun dhare piaar.
amrit velaa sach naau vadiaaee veechaar.
karmee aavai kapraa nadree mokh duaar.
naanak evai jaaneeai sabh aape sachiaar. ||4||
s1.english_meaning: The True Master has a true Name, spoken with boundless love. In the ambrosial hours of dawn, meditate on the True Name and contemplate His greatness; by grace comes the robe of honor, and by His glance of grace, the door of liberation is opened. O Nanak, know this: He Himself is the embodiment of truth.

s1.title: Japji Pauri 11 — Asankh Nav
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਅਸੰਖ ਨਾਵ ਅਸੰਖ ਥਾਵ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This declares the divine to be beyond counting, naming, and locating — then pivots to show that even letters and language are insufficient instruments. A learner gains a meditation on the limits of human description and the humility required to approach the Divine.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / Japji recitation / philosophical Sikh discourse
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਅਸੰਖ ਨਾਵ ਅਸੰਖ ਥਾਵ ॥
ਅਗੰਮ ਅਗੰਮ ਅਸੰਖ ਲੋਅ ॥
ਅਸੰਖ ਕਹਹਿ ਸਿਰਿ ਭਾਰੁ ਹੋਇ ॥
ਅਖਰੀ ਨਾਮੁ ਅਖਰੀ ਸਾਲਾਹ ॥
ਅਖਰੀ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਗੀਤ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਹ ॥
ਅਖਰੀ ਲਿਖਣੁ ਬੋਲਣੁ ਬਾਣਿ ॥
ਤੂ ਸਦਾ ਸਲਾਮਤਿ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ ॥੧੧॥
s1.romanization:
asankh naav asankh thaav.
agamm agamm asankh lo-a.
asankh kahehi sir bhaar hoe.
akhree naam akhree saalaah.
akhree giaan geet gun gaah.
akhree likhan bolan baan.
too sadaa salamat nirankaar. ||11||
s1.english_meaning: Innumerable are His names, innumerable His places; inaccessible, unreachable, and innumerable the worlds — to even say 'innumerable' becomes a burden. Through letters comes the Name, through letters His praise; through letters knowledge, songs, and the qualities of virtue; through letters writing and spoken speech. You are forever the Immortal, Formless One.

s1.title: Japji Pauri 34 — Dharamsaal
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਰਾਤੀ ਰੁਤੀ ਥਿਤੀ ਵਾਰ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This situates creation — nights, seasons, lunar dates, days, wind, water, fire — within a moral cosmos where the earth is a court of dharma and all beings are weighed by their deeds. A learner gains the Sikh view of ethical accountability within the natural world.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / katha on karma and creation / Japji teaching in schools
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਰਾਤੀ ਰੁਤੀ ਥਿਤੀ ਵਾਰ ॥
ਪਵਣ ਪਾਣੀ ਅਗਨੀ ਪਾਤਾਲ ॥
ਤਿਸੁ ਵਿਚਿ ਧਰਤੀ ਥਾਪਿ ਰਖੀ ਧਰਮ ਸਾਲ ॥
ਤਿਸੁ ਵਿਚਿ ਜੀਅ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਕੇ ਰੰਗ ॥
ਤਿਨ ਕੇ ਨਾਮ ਅਨੇਕ ਅਨੰਤ ॥
ਕਰਮੀ ਕਰਮੀ ਹੋਇ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
ਨਾਨਕ ਗਇਆ ਜਾਪੈ ਜਾਇ ॥੩੪॥
s1.romanization:
raatee rutee thitee vaar.
pavan paanee agnee paataal.
tis vich dhartee thaap rakhee dharam saal.
tis vich jeea jugat ke rang.
tin ke naam anek anant.
karmee karmee hoe veechaar.
naanak gaiaa jaapai jaae. ||34||
s1.english_meaning: Nights, seasons, lunar dates, and days; wind, water, fire, and the nether world — within all this, the earth is established as a court of dharma. Within it are beings in countless forms and colors, and their names are endless and infinite. By deeds, all are judged; O Nanak, the going is known only at the going.

s1.title: Japji Pauri 27 — Paataalaa Paataal
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਪਾਤਾਲਾ ਪਾਤਾਲ ਲਖ ਆਗਾਸਾ ਆਗਾਸ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This asserts the infinite extent of creation — depths below depths, heights above heights — beyond the reach of Vedas, scriptures, or counting. A learner gains a sense of cosmic scale that silences the ego and opens awe.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / cited in Sikh cosmology discussions / widely known in diaspora
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਪਾਤਾਲਾ ਪਾਤਾਲ ਲਖ ਆਗਾਸਾ ਆਗਾਸ ॥
ਓੜਕ ਓੜਕ ਭਾਲਿ ਥਕੇ ਵੇਦ ਕਹਨਿ ਇਕ ਵਾਤ ॥
ਸਹਸ ਅਠਾਰਹ ਕਹਨਿ ਕਤੇਬਾ ਅਸੁਲੂ ਇਕੁ ਧਾਤੁ ॥
ਲੇਖਾ ਹੋਇ ਤ ਲਿਖੀਐ ਲੇਖੈ ਹੋਇ ਵਿਣਾਸੁ ॥
ਨਾਨਕ ਵਡਾ ਆਖੀਐ ਆਪੇ ਜਾਣੈ ਆਪੁ ॥੨੭॥
s1.romanization:
paataalaa paataal lakh aagaasaa aagaas.
orhak orhak bhaal thake ved kahan ik vaat.
sahas athaarah kahan kateybaa asloo ik dhaat.
lekhaa hoe ta likheeai lekhai hoe vinaas.
naanak vadaa aakheeai aape jaanai aapu. ||27||
s1.english_meaning: Depths below depths, a hundred thousand and more; heights above heights and further still — the Vedas, exhausted in searching, say one thing; the Semitic scriptures speak of eighteen thousand worlds but the root is one. If an account could be written, it would be written — but in the accounting lies ruin. O Nanak, He is called great; He Himself knows Himself.

s1.title: Japji Pauri 38 — Jat Pahara
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਜਤੁ ਪਾਹਾਰਾ ਧੀਰਜੁ ਸੁਨਿਆਰੁ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This uses the extended metaphor of a forge: self-discipline is the hearth, patience the goldsmith, understanding the anvil, divine knowledge the tools, and love the vessel in which the Shabad is cast. A learner gains one of the most memorable craft-based ethical teachings in all of Gurbani.
s1.citation_signal: Nitnem / widely quoted in ethical discourse / Sikh camps and schools
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਜਤੁ ਪਾਹਾਰਾ ਧੀਰਜੁ ਸੁਨਿਆਰੁ ॥
ਅਹਰਣਿ ਮਤਿ ਵੇਦੁ ਹਥੀਆਰੁ ॥
ਭਉ ਖਲਾ ਅਗਨਿ ਤਪ ਤਾਉ ॥
ਭਾਂਡਾ ਭਾਉ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਤਿਤੁ ਢਾਲਿ ॥
ਘੜੀਐ ਸਬਦੁ ਸਚੀ ਟਕਸਾਲ ॥
ਜਿਨ ਕਉ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰਮੁ ਤਿਨ ਕਾਰ ॥
ਨਾਨਕ ਨਦਰੀ ਨਦਰਿ ਨਿਹਾਲ ॥੩੮॥
s1.romanization:
jat paahaaraa dheeraj suniaar.
ahran mat ved hathiaar.
bhau khalaa agni tap taau.
bhaandaa bhaau amrit tit dhaal.
gharheeai sabad sachee taksaal.
jin kaou nadar karam tin kaar.
naanak nadree nadar nihaal. ||38||
s1.english_meaning: Continence is the forge, patience the goldsmith; understanding is the anvil, divine knowledge the tool. Awe is the bellows, austerity the fire and heat; love is the vessel in which the nectar is poured, and in this true mint the Word is cast. Those upon whom grace falls — this is their work. O Nanak, by the glance of grace, one is filled with bliss.

s1.title: Asa di Var Pauri 1 — Divine Self-Creation
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਆਪੀਨ੍ਹੈ ਆਪੁ ਸਾਜਿਓਨੁ ਆਪੀਨ੍ਹੈ ਰਚਿਓ ਨਾਉ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This opens the great morning vaar by saying the Divine created Itself and then created Its own Name — establishing that creation is self-originating and the Name is coeval with the Creator. A learner gains a foundational Sikh cosmology statement heard in morning prayer.
s1.citation_signal: Asa di Var morning kirtan / widely recited in Gurudwaras / Sikh studies
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਆਪੀਨ੍ਹੈ ਆਪੁ ਸਾਜਿਓਨੁ ਆਪੀਨ੍ਹੈ ਰਚਿਓ ਨਾਉ ॥
ਦੁਯੀ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਸਾਜੀਐ ਕਰਿ ਆਸਣੁ ਡਿਠੋ ਚਾਉ ॥
ਦਾਤਾ ਕਰਤਾ ਆਪਿ ਤੂੰ ਤੁਸਿ ਦੇਵਹਿ ਕਰਹਿ ਪਸਾਉ ॥
ਤੂੰ ਜਾਣੋਈ ਸਭਸੈ ਦੇ ਲੈਸਹਿ ਜਿੰਦੁ ਕਵਾਉ ॥
ਕਰਿ ਆਸਣੁ ਡਿਠੋ ਚਾਉ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
aapeenhai aapu saajion aapeenhai rachio naau.
duyee kudrat saajeeai kar aasan ditho chaau.
daataa kartaa aap toon tus deveh kareh pasaau.
toon jaanoee sabhsai de laisahi jind kavaau.
kar aasan ditho chaau. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: The Divine fashioned Itself through Itself, and through Itself created the Name; second, creation was shaped, and seated on the throne the Creator gazed with delight. You Yourself are the Giver and Doer — pleased, You bestow Your grace. You are the one who knows all; You give life and take it back. Seated on the throne, You gaze with delight.

s1.title: Asa di Var Slok M2 — Guru Transforms
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਬਲਿਹਾਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਆਪਣੇ ਦਿਉਹਾੜੀ ਸਦਵਾਰ
s1.author: Guru Angad Dev
s1.why_dense: This salok declares the Guru as the one who transforms humans into angels in an instant, with sacrifice and devotion renewed again and again. A learner gains the core Sikh belief in the Guru as transformative force — not merely a teacher but a being who changes what one is.
s1.citation_signal: Asa di Var / morning kirtan / foundational Sikh Guru-devotion discourse
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਬਲਿਹਾਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਆਪਣੇ ਦਿਉਹਾੜੀ ਸਦਵਾਰ ॥
ਜਿਨਿ ਮਾਣਸ ਤੇ ਦੇਵਤੇ ਕੀਏ ਕਰਤ ਨ ਲਾਗੀ ਵਾਰ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
baliharee gur aapane diuhaarhee sadavaar.
jin maanas te devate keee karat na laagee vaar. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: I am a sacrifice to my Guru, again and again, day after day, forever — who transformed humans into angels in an instant.

s1.title: So Purkh
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਨਿਰੰਜਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਨਿਰੰਜਨੁ
s1.author: Guru Ram Das
s1.why_dense: This shabad praises the unstained, transcendent Being through a cascade of epithets and positions the speaker as a bride longing for the Spouse-Lord. A learner gains the bridal-mystical imagery central to Punjabi Sufi and Sikh devotion, heard in every evening Rehras Sahib.
s1.citation_signal: Rehras Sahib / evening Nitnem / Anand Karaj ceremony
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਸੋ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਨਿਰੰਜਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਨਿਰੰਜਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਅਗਮਾ ਅਗਮ ਅਪਾਰਾ ॥
ਸਭਿ ਧਿਆਵਹਿ ਸਭਿ ਧਿਆਵਹਿ ਤੁਧੁ ਜੀ ਹਰਿ ਸਚੇ ਸਿਰਜਣਹਾਰਾ ॥
ਸਭਿ ਜੀਅ ਤੁਮਾਰੇ ਜੀ ਤੂੰ ਜੀਆ ਕਾ ਦਾਤਾਰਾ ॥
ਹਰਿ ਧਿਆਵਹੁ ਸੰਤਹੁ ਜੀ ਸਭਿ ਦੂਖ ਵਿਸਾਰਣਹਾਰਾ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
so purakh niranjan har purakh niranjan har agamaa agam apaaraa.
sabh dhiaavahi sabh dhiaavahi tudh jee har sache sirjanhaaraa.
sabh jeea tumaarey jee toon jeeaa kaa daataaraa.
har dhiaavahu santahu jee sabh dookh visaaranhaaaraa. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: That Being, unstained — all meditate on You, O True Creator, unreachable and unbounded. All souls are Yours; You are the Giver to all souls. O saints, meditate on the Lord, who erases all sorrows.

s1.title: Kirtan Sohila Opening
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਜੈ ਘਰਿ ਕੀਰਤਿ ਆਖੀਐ ਕਰਤੇ ਕਾ ਹੋਇ ਬੀਚਾਰੁ
s1.author: Guru Nanak
s1.why_dense: This opens the bedtime prayer by describing the home where divine praise is sung as one where the Creator's glory is contemplated. A learner gains the mood and vocabulary of Sikh evening ritual — intimacy, the invitation to make the home a sacred space.
s1.citation_signal: Kirtan Sohila / bedtime Nitnem / antim ardas recitation at funerals
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਜੈ ਘਰਿ ਕੀਰਤਿ ਆਖੀਐ ਕਰਤੇ ਕਾ ਹੋਇ ਬੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
ਤਿਤੁ ਘਰਿ ਗਾਵਹੁ ਸੋਹਿਲਾ ਸਿਵਰਿਹੁ ਸਿਰਜਣਹਾਰੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
ਤੁਮ ਗਾਵਹੁ ਮੇਰੇ ਨਿਰਭਉ ਕਾ ਸੋਹਿਲਾ ॥
ਹਉ ਵਾਰੀ ਜਿਤੁ ਸੋਹਿਲੈ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੋਇ ॥
s1.romanization:
jai ghar keerti aakheeai karte kaa hoe beechaar.
tit ghar gaavahu sohilaa sivrihu sirjanhaar. ||1|| rahaao ||
tum gaavahu mere nirbhau kaa sohilaa.
hau vaaree jit sohilai sadaa sukh hoe.
s1.english_meaning: In that home where the Creator's glory is praised and His qualities contemplated, there sing the song of praise and remember the Creator. O my beloved, sing the song of praise of the Fearless One — I am a sacrifice to that song of praise in which eternal peace abides.

s1.title: Lavan 1 — First Circumambulation
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਪਹਿਲੜੀ ਲਾਵ ਪਰਵਿਰਤੀ ਕਰਮ ਦ੍ਰਿੜਾਇਆ ਬਲਿ ਰਾਮ ਜੀਉ
s1.author: Guru Ram Das
s1.why_dense: This describes the first circumambulation of the Anand Karaj wedding ceremony as a turn toward righteous action, family duty, and the Guru's teaching. A learner gains the ceremonial Punjabi that every Sikh couple walks to and understands the spiritual dimension of Sikh marriage.
s1.citation_signal: Anand Karaj wedding ceremony / heard by every Sikh family / cultural literacy
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਹਰਿ ਪਹਿਲੜੀ ਲਾਵ ਪਰਵਿਰਤੀ ਕਰਮ ਦ੍ਰਿੜਾਇਆ ਬਲਿ ਰਾਮ ਜੀਉ ॥
ਬਾਣੀ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾ ਵੇਦਾ ਧਰਮੁ ਦ੍ਰਿੜਹੁ ਪਾਪ ਤਜਾਇਆ ਬਲਿ ਰਾਮ ਜੀਉ ॥
ਧਰਮੁ ਦ੍ਰਿੜਹੁ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਵਹੁ ਸਿਮ੍ਰਿਤਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਦ੍ਰਿੜਾਇਆ ॥
ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਗੁਰੁ ਪੂਰਾ ਆਰਾਧਹੁ ਸਭਿ ਕਿਲਵਿਖ ਪਾਪ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥
s1.romanization:
har pehlrhee laav parvirtee karam drirhaaiaa bal raam jeeo.
baanee brahmaa vedaa dharam drirhu paap tajaaiaa bal raam jeeo.
dharam drirhu har naam dhiaavahu simrit naam drirhaaiaa.
satigur gur pooraa aaraadahu sabh kilvikh paap gavaaiaa.
s1.english_meaning: In the first round of the wedding circumambulation, the Lord sets out the path of righteous action: embrace the sacred word, hold to dharma and forsake sin. Meditate on the divine Name, hold it firmly in memory; worship the perfect True Guru, and all sins and faults are removed.

s1.title: Lavan 4 — Fourth Circumambulation
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਚਉਥੜੀ ਲਾਵ ਮਨਿ ਸਹਜੁ ਭਇਆ ਹਰਿ ਪਾਇਆ ਬਲਿ ਰਾਮ ਜੀਉ
s1.author: Guru Ram Das
s1.why_dense: This describes the fourth and final circumambulation as the moment of spiritual union — mind finds equipoise, the Divine is found, the soul-bride merges with the Beloved. A learner gains the climactic language of Sikh mystical union expressed through wedding ritual.
s1.citation_signal: Anand Karaj / peak of the wedding ceremony / widely known in Sikh cultural life
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਹਰਿ ਚਉਥੜੀ ਲਾਵ ਮਨਿ ਸਹਜੁ ਭਇਆ ਹਰਿ ਪਾਇਆ ਬਲਿ ਰਾਮ ਜੀਉ ॥
ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਿਲਿਆ ਸੁਭਾਇ ਹਰਿ ਮਨਿ ਤਨਿ ਮੀਠਾ ਲਾਇਆ ਬਲਿ ਰਾਮ ਜੀਉ ॥
ਹਰਿ ਮੀਠਾ ਲਾਇਆ ਮੇਰੇ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਭਾਇਆ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈ ॥
ਮਨ ਚਿੰਦਿਆ ਫਲੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਵਜੀ ਵਾਧਾਈ ॥
s1.romanization:
har chautrhee laav man sahj bhaiaa har paaiaa bal raam jeeo.
gurmukh miliaa subhaae har man tan meethaa laaiaa bal raam jeeo.
har meethaa laaiaa mere prabh bhaaiaa andin har liv laaee.
man chindiaa phal paaiaa suaamee har naam vajee vaadhaaee.
s1.english_meaning: In the fourth round, the mind finds equipoise — the Lord is found. Through the Gurumukh, the Lord naturally dwells in mind and body, sweet and beloved; night and day, the mind remains absorbed in the Lord. The desired fruit is obtained, O Master, and the horn of celebration sounds in the Name.

s1.title: Sukhmani — Prabh Kaa Simran Sab Te Uchaa
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੈ ਸਿਮਰਨਿ ਸਭ ਤੇ ਊਚਾ
s1.author: Guru Arjan Dev
s1.why_dense: This declares divine remembrance to be the highest of all activities — surpassing rites, austerities, and knowledge — because it transforms the inner state completely. A learner gains the foundational Sukhmani teaching that makes simran central to Sikh practice.
s1.citation_signal: Sukhmani Sahib / widely memorized / school and camp teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੈ ਸਿਮਰਨਿ ਸਭ ਤੇ ਊਚਾ ॥
ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੈ ਸਿਮਰਨਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਸਭੁ ਕੀਚਾ ॥
ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੈ ਸਿਮਰਨਿ ਕਾਰਜ ਪੂਰੇ ॥
ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੈ ਸਿਮਰਨਿ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਦੂਰੇ ॥
s1.romanization:
prabh kai simran sabh te oochaa.
prabh kai simran sukh sabh keechaa.
prabh kai simran kaaraj poore.
prabh kai simran durmat doore.
s1.english_meaning: In the remembrance of God, all things are made exalted; in the remembrance of God, all peace is secured; in the remembrance of God, all tasks are fulfilled; in the remembrance of God, perverse thinking is banished.

s1.title: Sukhmani — Sewa Karat Hoi Nihkaamee
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਸੇਵਾ ਕਰਤ ਹੋਇ ਨਿਹਕਾਮੀ
s1.author: Guru Arjan Dev
s1.why_dense: This teaches that service performed without desire for reward leads to liberation in this very life. A learner gains the Sikh concept of nihkaam sewa — practically actionable in daily life and directly tied to the Guru's path.
s1.citation_signal: Sukhmani Sahib / widely quoted in langar and sewa discourse / Sikh teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਸੇਵਾ ਕਰਤ ਹੋਇ ਨਿਹਕਾਮੀ ॥
ਤਿਸ ਕਉ ਹੋਤ ਪਰਾਪਤਿ ਸੁਆਮੀ ॥
ਅਪਨੀ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਜਿਸੁ ਆਪਿ ਕਰੇਇ ॥
ਨਾਨਕ ਸੋ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਮਤਿ ਲੇਇ ॥
s1.romanization:
sevaa karat hoe nihkaamee.
tis kaou hot paraapt suaamee.
apanee kirpaa jis aap karee.
naanak so sevak gur kee mat lee.
s1.english_meaning: One who performs service without desire attains the Master. On whom He Himself bestows His grace — O Nanak, that servant takes the Guru's wisdom.

s1.title: Sukhmani — Jis Simarat Dukh Lahe
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਸਿਮਰਤ ਦੁਖੁ ਲਹੈ
s1.author: Guru Arjan Dev
s1.why_dense: This teaches that remembering the Divine removes sorrow and brings contentment in the present moment, making simran a practical emotional resource, not merely a religious discipline. A learner gains language for the healing function of spiritual practice.
s1.citation_signal: Sukhmani Sahib / grief counseling contexts / widely quoted in hardship
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਜਿਸੁ ਸਿਮਰਤ ਦੁਖੁ ਲਹੈ ॥
ਜਿਸੁ ਸਿਮਰਤ ਸੁਖੁ ਰਹੈ ॥
ਜਿਸੁ ਸਿਮਰਤ ਭਉ ਨ ਸਤਾਏ ॥
ਨਾਨਕ ਸੋ ਮਿਤ੍ਰੁ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਰੂਪ ਦਿਖਾਏ ॥
s1.romanization:
jis simrat dukh lahai.
jis simrat sukh rahai.
jis simrat bhau na sataae.
naanak so mitr suaamee roop dikhaae.
s1.english_meaning: Remembering whom, sorrow departs; remembering whom, peace remains; remembering whom, fear does not torment — O Nanak, that Friend, the Master, reveals His form.

s1.title: Sukhmani — Saadh Kai Sang
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਸਾਧ ਕੈ ਸੰਗਿ ਮਲੁ ਸਗਲੀ ਖੋਵੈ
s1.author: Guru Arjan Dev
s1.why_dense: This ashtapadi describes the company of the saint (saadh sangat) as transformative: all filth is removed, perverse thinking subsides, and divine virtue is cultivated. A learner gains the theological basis for why Sikhs gather in sangat and the spiritual mechanics attributed to it.
s1.citation_signal: Sukhmani Sahib / sangat teaching / widely cited in community and service discourse
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਸਾਧ ਕੈ ਸੰਗਿ ਮਲੁ ਸਗਲੀ ਖੋਵੈ ॥
ਸਾਧ ਕੈ ਸੰਗਿ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਮਲੁ ਧੋਵੈ ॥
ਸਾਧ ਕੈ ਸੰਗਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਿ ਹਰਿ ਕੇਰਾ ॥
ਸਾਧ ਕੈ ਸੰਗਿ ਮਿਲਿਆ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਮੇਰਾ ॥
s1.romanization:
saadh kai sang mal saglee khovai.
saadh kai sang durmat mal dhovai.
saadh kai sang naam jap har keraa.
saadh kai sang miliaa prabh meraa.
s1.english_meaning: In the company of the saint, all filth is removed; in the company of the saint, the grime of perverse thinking is washed away. In the company of the saint, one meditates on the Lord's Name; in the company of the saint, my God is found.

s1.title: Salok M9 — Kaahu Ko Dosh Na Deo
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਕਾਹੂ ਕੋ ਦੋਸੁ ਨ ਦੇਊ
s1.author: Guru Tegh Bahadur
s1.why_dense: This teaches radical personal responsibility: do not blame others, for all that happens is the fruit of one's own past karma. A learner gains a simple ethical anchor that redirects attention inward rather than projecting blame outward.
s1.citation_signal: Salok M9 / ethical discourse / widely quoted in katha and counseling
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਕਾਹੂ ਕੋ ਦੋਸੁ ਨ ਦੇਊ ਨਾਨਕ ਖਾਲਕੁ ਸਭ ਮੈ ਰੇਊ ॥
ਆਪਨੀ ਕਰਣੀ ਆਪ ਬੀਚਾਰੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਿ ਸੁਖ ਸਾਰੁ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
kaahoo ko dos na deoo naanak khaalak sabh mai reoo.
aapanee karnee aap beechaar naanak naam jap sukh saar. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: Do not blame anyone, O Nanak — the Creator pervades all. Reflect on your own deeds; O Nanak, meditate on the Name — that is the essence of peace.

s1.title: Salok M9 — Raam Naam Ur Meh Gahio
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ਉਰਿ ਮੈ ਗਹਿਓ
s1.author: Guru Tegh Bahadur
s1.why_dense: This salok describes holding the divine name in the heart as the sole protection amid the vicissitudes of worldly existence. A learner gains a compact devotional instruction delivered with Guru Tegh Bahadur's characteristic quietude and directness.
s1.citation_signal: Salok M9 / Nitnem additions / daily simran teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ਉਰਿ ਮੈ ਗਹਿਓ ਜਾ ਕੈ ਸਮ ਨਹਿ ਕੋਇ ॥
ਜਿਹ ਸਿਮਰਤ ਸੰਕਟ ਮਿਟੈ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਹੋਤ ਸੁ ਹੋਇ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
raam naam ur mai gahio jaa kai sam neh koe.
jih simrat sankat mitai darsan hot su hoe. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: I have grasped the Lord's Name in my heart — there is none equal to it. Remembering it, all troubles vanish, and the vision of the Lord is attained.

s1.title: Salok M9 — Jo Upjio So Binas Hai
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਉਪਜਿਓ ਸੋ ਬਿਨਸਿ ਹੈ ਪਰੋ ਆਜੁ ਕੈ ਕਾਲਿ
s1.author: Guru Tegh Bahadur
s1.why_dense: This salok states plainly: whatever has arisen will perish, today or tomorrow. A learner gains the core Sikh teaching on impermanence that underpins detachment — spoken by the Guru who faced martyrdom with equanimity.
s1.citation_signal: Salok M9 / bereavement and mortality discourse / widely quoted in katha
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਜੋ ਉਪਜਿਓ ਸੋ ਬਿਨਸਿ ਹੈ ਪਰੋ ਆਜੁ ਕੈ ਕਾਲਿ ॥
ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਇ ਲੇ ਛਾਡਿ ਸਗਲ ਜੰਜਾਲ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
jo upjio so binas hai paro aaju kai kaal.
naanak har gun gaae le chhaad sagal janjaal. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: Whatever has arisen will perish — perhaps today, perhaps tomorrow. O Nanak, sing the praises of the Lord and abandon all entanglements.

s1.title: Farida — Khaaku Na Nindeeai
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਖਾਕੁ ਨ ਨਿੰਦੀਐ ਖਾਕੂ ਜੇਡੁ ਨ ਕੋਇ
s1.author: Baba Farid
s1.why_dense: Farid says: do not despise dust — for there is none greater than dust. The living walk upon it; the dead are laid within it; one who was alive shall return to it. A learner gains a remarkable inversion of hierarchy and a meditation on mortality in simple Punjabi.
s1.citation_signal: Salok Sheikh Farid / widely cited in funeral and humility discourse / Punjabi culture
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਫਰੀਦਾ ਖਾਕੁ ਨ ਨਿੰਦੀਐ ਖਾਕੂ ਜੇਡੁ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥
ਜੀਵਦਿਆ ਪੈਰਾ ਤਲੈ ਮੁਇਆ ਉਪਰਿ ਹੋਇ ॥੨੧॥
s1.romanization:
Fareedaa khaak na nindeeai khaakoo jed na koe.
jeevadiaa pairaa talai muiaa upar hoe. ||21||
s1.english_meaning: Farid says: do not despise dust — for there is none greater than dust. The living walk upon it underfoot; when dead, they lie beneath it.

s1.title: Farida — Jo Main Kitiaa
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜੋ ਮੈ ਕੀਤਾ ਸੋ ਮੈ ਪਾਇਆ ਦੋਸੁ ਨ ਦੇਊ ਕਿਸੈ
s1.author: Baba Farid
s1.why_dense: Farid says: what I did, I received — I blame no one else. A learner gains one of the most direct statements of moral accountability in SGGS: cause and effect expressed as personal responsibility, not cosmic punishment.
s1.citation_signal: Salok Sheikh Farid / widely quoted in ethical discourse / school and katha use
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜੋ ਮੈ ਕੀਤਾ ਸੋ ਮੈ ਪਾਇਆ ਦੋਸੁ ਨ ਦੇਊ ਕਿਸੈ ॥
ਜੋ ਬੀਜੈ ਸੋ ਲੁਣੈ ਇਹੁ ਅਚਰਜੁ ਦੇਖੁ ਤਿਸੈ ॥੬੬॥
s1.romanization:
Fareedaa jo mai keetaa so mai paaiaa dos na deoo kisai.
jo beejai so lunai ih acharaj dekhu tisai. ||66||
s1.english_meaning: Farid says: what I did, I received — I do not blame anyone else. What one sows, one reaps — behold this wonder.

s1.title: Farida — Jo Tain Maaran Mukeeyaan
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜੋ ਤੈ ਮਾਰਨਿ ਮੁਕੀਆਂ ਤਿਨ੍ਹ੍ਹਾ ਨ ਮਾਰੇ ਘੁੰਮਿ
s1.author: Baba Farid
s1.why_dense: Farid teaches: those who strike you with their fists — do not strike them back; go home and kiss their feet. One of the most provocative non-retaliation teachings in any scripture. A learner gains Farid's radical ethics of forbearance that shocked even medieval contemporaries.
s1.citation_signal: Salok Sheikh Farid / interfaith ethics discourse / widely cited in Sikh and Sufi teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜੋ ਤੈ ਮਾਰਨਿ ਮੁਕੀਆਂ ਤਿਨ੍ਹ੍ਹਾ ਨ ਮਾਰੇ ਘੁੰਮਿ ॥
ਆਪਨੜੈ ਘਰਿ ਜਾਇਏ ਪਇਰ ਤਿਨ੍ਹ੍ਹਾ ਦੇ ਚੁੰਮਿ ॥੭॥
s1.romanization:
Fareedaa jo tai maaran mukeeyaan tinhaa na maare ghum.
aapanrhai ghar jaaie pair tinhaa de chum. ||7||
s1.english_meaning: Farid says: those who strike you with their fists — do not strike them back. Go home and kiss their feet.

s1.title: Ravidas — Begampura
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਬੇਗਮ ਪੁਰਾ ਸਹਰ ਕੋ ਨਾਉ
s1.author: Ravidas
s1.why_dense: Ravidas describes a city without sorrow where there is no tax, no suffering, no fear, and no second class — a utopian vision of liberation written by a low-caste poet. A learner gains radical social theology that anticipates egalitarianism and challenges caste oppression.
s1.citation_signal: widely cited in Dalit and Sikh social justice discourse / school and academic use / literary anthologies
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਬੇਗਮ ਪੁਰਾ ਸਹਰ ਕੋ ਨਾਉ ॥
ਦੁਖੁ ਅੰਦੋਹੁ ਨਹੀ ਤਿਹਿ ਠਾਉ ॥
ਨਾਂ ਤਸਵੀਸ ਖਿਰਾਜੁ ਨ ਮਾਲੁ ॥
ਖਉਫੁ ਨ ਖਤਾ ਨ ਤਰਸੁ ਜਵਾਲੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
s1.romanization:
begam puraa sahar ko naau.
dukh andohu nahee tihi thaau.
naan tasavees khiraaj na maal.
khauf na khataa na taras javaal. ||1|| rahaao ||
s1.english_meaning: Begampura — City Without Sorrow — is its name. There is no pain or grief in that place; no anxiety, no taxes, no property, no fear, no sin, no falling into the pit of affliction.

s1.title: Ravidas — Tum Chandan Ham Paanee
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਤੁਮ ਚੰਦਨ ਹਮ ਪਾਣੀ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਤੁਮ ਚੰਦਨ ਹਮ ਪਾਣੀ
s1.author: Ravidas
s1.why_dense: Ravidas compares himself to water absorbing the fragrance of sandalwood (the Divine) through proximity — devotion as transformative contact rather than ritual merit. A learner gains the Bhakti theology of closeness to the Divine as the mechanism of spiritual elevation.
s1.citation_signal: Ravidas bani in SGGS / Bhakti movement teaching / interfaith discourse
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਤੁਮ ਚੰਦਨ ਹਮ ਪਾਣੀ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਤੁਮ ਚੰਦਨ ਹਮ ਪਾਣੀ ॥
ਅੰਗਿ ਅੰਗਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਸੁਗੰਧੀ ਸਮਾਣੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
ਤੁਮ ਘਨ ਬਨ ਹਮ ਮੋਰ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਤੁਮ ਘਨ ਬਨ ਹਮ ਮੋਰਾ ॥
ਨਿਸਿ ਦਿਨੁ ਨਾਮ ਸਿਮਰਿ ਤੇਰੋ ਤਉ ਜਿਉ ਚਾਤ੍ਰਿਕ ਚਕੋਰਾ ॥
s1.romanization:
tum chandan ham paanee saahib tum chandan ham paanee.
ang ang mil rahiaa sugandee samaanee. ||1|| rahaao ||
tum ghan ban ham mor suaamee tum ghan ban hammoraa.
nis din naam simar tero tau jiu chaatrik chakoraa.
s1.english_meaning: You are the sandalwood, I am the water, O Master — limb by limb we are suffused together, the fragrance spreading through me. You are the clouds and the forest, I am the peacock, O Master; day and night I remember Your Name, like the chaatrik bird and the chakora longing for rain and moonlight.

s1.title: Kabir — Saakat Sang Na Keejiai
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰ ਸਾਕਤ ਸੰਗੁ ਨਾ ਕੀਜੀਐ
s1.author: Kabir
s1.why_dense: Kabir warns: do not keep company with the god-averse — even a brief touch contaminates, like a pot smeared with oil. A learner gains a brisk moral instruction about spiritual company delivered in Kabir's characteristic folk directness.
s1.citation_signal: widely quoted in Sikh teaching / sangat and satsang discourse / school curricula
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਕਬੀਰ ਸਾਕਤ ਸੰਗੁ ਨਾ ਕੀਜੀਐ ਦੂਰਹਿ ਜਾਈਐ ਭਾਗਿ ॥
ਬਾਸਨੁ ਕਾਰੋ ਪਰਸੀਐ ਕਾਰੋ ਹੀ ਹੋਇ ਲਾਗਿ ॥੧੩੧॥
s1.romanization:
kabeer saakat sang na keejeeai dooreh jaaeeai bhaag.
baasan kaaro parseeai kaaro hee hoe laag. ||131||
s1.english_meaning: Kabir says: do not keep company with the god-averse — flee far away from them. Touch a smeared pot and the black rubs off on you.

s1.title: Kabir — Pothi Parmesar Kaa Thaan
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਪੋਥੀ ਪਰਮੇਸਰ ਕਾ ਥਾਨੁ
s1.author: Kabir
s1.why_dense: Kabir declares scripture to be the very dwelling place of the Divine — whoever reads and understands it finds liberation. A learner gains Kabir's affirmation of the Gurbani as a living divine presence, connecting literacy, devotion, and liberation.
s1.citation_signal: Kabir bani in SGGS / book literacy in Sikh tradition / quoted in gurmat teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਪੋਥੀ ਪਰਮੇਸਰ ਕਾ ਥਾਨੁ ॥
ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਗਾਵਹਿ ਗੁਣ ਗਿਆਨੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
ਸਾਧੂ ਕੈ ਮਨਿ ਬਸਤਿ ਗੁਪਾਲੁ ॥
ਜਨ ਕਾ ਦੇਖਿਆ ਸਭੁ ਸਾਲੁ ॥
s1.romanization:
pothee parmesar kaa thaan.
saadhsang gaavahi gun giaan. ||1|| rahaao ||
saadhoo kai man basat gopaal.
jan kaa dekhiaa sabh saal.
s1.english_meaning: Scripture is the dwelling place of the Supreme Lord; in the company of the holy, the knowledge of His virtues is sung. In the saint's mind dwells the Sustainer of the World; all that the humble servant beholds is auspicious.

s1.title: Namdev — Gobind Gobind Gobind Sang
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਸੰਗਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਤ ਦਨੋ ਭਈ ਲਾਖਪਤਿ
s1.author: Namdev
s1.why_dense: Namdev declares that by repeating the divine name Gobind while doing his daily labor of textile printing, a lowly weaver became spiritually wealthy. A learner gains the Bhakti teaching that any work becomes spiritual when done in remembrance of the Divine.
s1.citation_signal: Namdev bani in SGGS / labor and devotion discourse / Bhakti tradition teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਸੰਗਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਤ ਦਨੋ ਭਈ ਲਾਖਪਤਿ ॥
ਧਨੁ ਰਾਸਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਅਗਨਿ ਬੁਝੀ ਹਰਿ ਜਪਤ ਸੁਤ ਪਰਭਤਿ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
ਊਚ ਤੇ ਊਚ ਭਗਵੰਤ ਅਮਰ ਨਾਮਦੇਉ ਮਨਿ ਰਮਤਿ ॥
ਸਮਦਰਸੀ ਭੇਟੇ ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਤਉ ਮਿਲੇ ਜਗਜੀਵਨ ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ॥
s1.romanization:
gobind gobind gobind sang naam japat dano bhaee laakhpat.
dhan raas maaiaa agni bujhee har japat sut parbhat. ||1|| rahaao ||
ooch te ooch bhagvant amar naamdeou man ramat.
samdarasee bhete saadhasang tau mile jagjeevan abigat.
s1.english_meaning: Repeating 'Gobind Gobind Gobind' in the company of the Name, the weaver Namdev became wealthy. The fire of craving was quenched, wealth and wealth-illusion exhausted, meditating on the Lord at dawn. The highest of the high is the Immortal Lord — Namdev's mind dwells in Him; meeting the even-sighted One in the company of the holy, the Immortal Life of the world is found.

s1.title: Guru Ram Das — Mere Man Prem Lago Har Teer
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੇ ਮਨਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮੁ ਲਗੋ ਹਰਿ ਤੀਰ
s1.author: Guru Ram Das
s1.why_dense: This shabad describes the mind struck by the arrow of divine love — longing, absorption, and surrender. A learner gains the signature affective register of Guru Ram Das's bani: helpless love, yearning, and union through vivid Punjabi imagery.
s1.citation_signal: Guru Ram Das bani / love-register kirtan / widely sung in Gurudwaras
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਮੇਰੇ ਮਨਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮੁ ਲਗੋ ਹਰਿ ਤੀਰ ॥
ਹਉ ਰਹਿ ਨ ਸਕਉ ਬਿਨੁ ਦੇਖੇ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮ ਨੈਣ ਭਏ ਅਤਿ ਤੀਰ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
ਤਨੁ ਮਨੁ ਸੀਤਲੁ ਭਇਆ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਪਾਇ ਮਨਿ ਭਈ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਿ ਸਬੂਰ ॥
ਹਰਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਾਮੁ ਪੀਆਲਾ ਦੀਆ ਪੀ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਿ ਰਹੇ ਮਨ ਕੀ ਭੂਰ ॥
s1.romanization:
mere man prem lago har teer.
hau reh na sakau bin dekhe preetam nain bhaee at teer. ||1|| rahaao ||
tan man seetal bhaiaa darasan paae man bhaee tripta saboor.
har amrit naam peaalaa deeaa pee tripat rahe man kee bhoor.
s1.english_meaning: My mind has been pierced by love for the Lord; I cannot bear to go without beholding my Beloved — my eyes are sharp with longing. Body and mind grow cool at the vision of the Lord; the mind is satisfied and content. He gave me the cup of the Lord's nectar-Name; drinking it, the cravings of the mind were quenched.

s1.title: Guru Ram Das — Gur Ki Murat Mann Meh Dhiaan
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਮਨ ਮਹਿ ਧਿਆਨੁ
s1.author: Guru Ram Das
s1.why_dense: This shabad teaches meditating on the Guru's form in the mind, the Guru's feet in the heart, and the Guru's word on the lips — a three-part devotional practice. A learner gains a compact, practical instruction for Guru-centric Sikh devotion.
s1.citation_signal: Guru Ram Das bani / Guru devotion discourse / widely taught in Sikh education
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਮਨ ਮਹਿ ਧਿਆਨੁ ॥
ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਚਰਨਿ ਮਨੁ ਕਰਉ ਇਸਨਾਨੁ ॥
ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਬਾਣੀ ਮੁਖਿ ਸਦਾ ਬਖਾਨੁ ॥
ਗੁਰੁ ਪੂਰਾ ਮਿਲਿਆ ਭਗਵਾਨੁ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
gur kee moorat man meh dhiaan.
gur kai charan man karaou isnaan.
gur kee baanee mukh sadaa bakhaan.
gur pooraa miliaa bhagvaan. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: Meditate on the Guru's form in the mind; bathe the mind at the Guru's feet; always recite the Guru's Word from the lips — through the perfect Guru, God is found.

s1.title: Guru Amar Das — Haumai Deeragh Rog Hai
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਦੀਰਘ ਰੋਗੁ ਹੈ ਦਾਰੂ ਭੀ ਇਸੁ ਮਾਹਿ
s1.author: Guru Amar Das
s1.why_dense: This salok states that ego is a long chronic illness — but its cure is also contained within it: the Name is the medicine. A learner gains the central Sikh teaching on haumai (ego) as the root problem and simran as the paradoxical solution.
s1.citation_signal: Asa di Var / widely quoted in Sikh philosophical discourse / foundational spiritual teaching
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਹਉਮੈ ਦੀਰਘ ਰੋਗੁ ਹੈ ਦਾਰੂ ਭੀ ਇਸੁ ਮਾਹਿ ॥
ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰੇ ਜੇ ਆਪਣੀ ਤਾ ਗੁਰ ਕਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਕਮਾਹਿ ॥੨॥
s1.romanization:
haumai deeragh rog hai daaroo bhee is maahi.
kirpaa kare je aapanee taa gur kaa sabad kamaahi. ||2||
s1.english_meaning: Ego is a long and chronic illness — yet the cure is also within it. When He bestows His grace, one practices the Guru's Word.

s1.title: Guru Amar Das — Eko Naam Hukam Hai
s1.first_line_gurmukhi: ਏਕੋ ਨਾਮੁ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਹੈ ਨਾਨਕ ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਦੀਆ ਬੁਝਾਇ
s1.author: Guru Amar Das
s1.why_dense: This salok states that the one Name is the Hukam (divine command) and this understanding was given by the True Guru — synthesizing devotion, obedience, and grace in a single couplet. A learner gains the Sikh synthesis of Name, will, and guidance in the most compact possible form.
s1.citation_signal: Guru Amar Das bani / widely cited in Sikh theology / katha and philosophical discourse
s1.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਏਕੋ ਨਾਮੁ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਹੈ ਨਾਨਕ ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਦੀਆ ਬੁਝਾਇ ॥
ਮੁਕਤਿ ਭੁਗਤਿ ਅਤੇ ਸੁਰਗ ਸੀ ਜਾਤੀ ਸਭੁ ਨਾਮਿ ਸਮਾਇ ॥੧॥
s1.romanization:
eko naam hukam hai naanak satigur deeaa bujhaae.
mukat bhugat ate surag see jaatee sabh naam samaae. ||1||
s1.english_meaning: The one Name is the Hukam (divine command) — O Nanak, the True Guru has given this understanding. Liberation, fulfillment, and even heaven's bliss — all are absorbed in the Name.

s2.title: Who Am I?
s2.why_dense: This is the classic Punjabi Sufi statement of unfixing identity through negation of labels, roles, and inherited categories. A learner gains core vocabulary of selfhood, religion, and social naming while encountering perhaps the single most quoted line in Punjabi mystical culture.
s2.cultural_signal: widely quoted in diaspora; Punjabi folk music; qawwali tradition; Sufi mehfil
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਬੁੱਲ੍ਹਾ ਕੀ ਜਾਣਾ ਮੈਂ ਕੌਣ
ਨਾ ਮੈਂ ਮੋਮਨ ਵਿਚ ਮਸੀਤਾਂ
ਨਾ ਮੈਂ ਵਿਚ ਕੁਫਰ ਦੀਆਂ ਰੀਤਾਂ
ਨਾ ਮੈਂ ਪਾਕਾਂ ਵਿਚ ਪਲੀਤਾਂ
ਨਾ ਮੈਂ ਮੂਸਾ ਨਾ ਫਿਰਔਣ
s2.romanization:
Bullha kee jaana main kaun
Naa main moman vich maseetaan
Naa main vich kufar diyaan reetaan
Naa main paakaan vich paleetaan
Naa main Moosaa naa Firaun
s2.english_meaning: Bulleh says: how should I know who I am? I am not contained among the faithful in mosques, nor in the customs of unbelief; neither among the pure nor the impure, neither Moses nor Pharaoh. The voice stands outside all oppositions, seeking the self beyond every religious and worldly label.

s2.title: Sisters Came to Advise Bulleh
s2.first_line_gurmukhi: ਬੁੱਲ੍ਹੇ ਨੂੰ ਸਮਝਾਵਣ ਆਈਆਂ ਭੈਣਾਂ ਤੇ ਭਰਜਾਈਆਂ
s2.why_dense: This poem dramatizes social pressure, family honor, and spiritual defiance through a vivid domestic scene voiced in everyday Punjabi. A learner gains kinship vocabulary and insight into how Sufi poetry uses feminine speech to critique conformity and caste-respectability.
s2.cultural_signal: Punjabi folk music; Sufi mehfil; widely cited in discussions of social rebellion
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਬੁੱਲ੍ਹੇ ਨੂੰ ਸਮਝਾਵਣ ਆਈਆਂ ਭੈਣਾਂ ਤੇ ਭਰਜਾਈਆਂ,
ਮੰਨ ਲੈ ਬੁੱਲ੍ਹਿਆ ਸਾਡਾ ਕਹਿਣਾ, ਛੱਡ ਦੇ ਪੱਲਾ ਰਾਈਆਂ.
ਆਲ ਨਬੀ ਔਲਾਦ ਨਬੀ ਨੂੰ ਤੂੰ ਕਿਉਂ ਲੀਕਾਂ ਲਾਈਆਂ,
ਜੇੜਾ ਸਾਨੂੰ ਸਈਦ ਸਦਾਵੇ, ਦੋਜ਼ਖ ਮਿਲਣ ਸਜ਼ਾਈਆਂ.
ਬੁੱਲ੍ਹਾ ਸ਼ਾਹ ਅਸਾਂ ਮਰਨਾ ਨਾਹੀਂ, ਗੋਰ ਪਿਆ ਕੋਈ ਹੋਰ.
s2.romanization:
Bulleh noon samjhaavan aaiyaan bhainaan te bharjaaiyaan,
mann lai Bullhiaa saada kahinaa, chhadd de palla Raaiyaan.
Aal Nabi aulaad Nabi noon toon kyon leekaan laaiyaan,
jehraa saanoon Saiyad sadaave, dozakh milan sazaaiyaan.
Bullha Shah asaan marnaa naaheen, gor piaa koee hor.
s2.english_meaning: Bulleh’s sisters and sisters-in-law come to counsel him: accept our word, they say, and let go the hem of the Arain master. Why do you draw lines against the Prophet’s own lineage, and call yourself by another bond? Yet Bulleh answers from the station beyond caste and grave: it is not I who die—someone else lies buried in the tomb.

s2.title: Neither Hindu nor Muslim
s2.why_dense: This kafi condenses Punjabi Sufi non-dualism into a direct refusal of communal identity as the final truth of the self. A learner gains interfaith vocabulary and a foundational text for understanding the Sufi-Bhakti continuum in Punjab.
s2.cultural_signal: widely quoted in diaspora; interfaith events; Sufi mehfil; school and university citation
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਹਿੰਦੂ ਨਾ ਨਹੀਂ ਮੁਸਲਮਾਨ,
ਬੈਠਾ ਤ੍ਰਿੰਜਣ ਧਰਮ ਸ਼ਾਲਾ.
ਸੁੰਨੀ ਨਾ ਨਹੀਂ ਅਸੀਂ ਸ਼ੀਆ,
ਸੁਲਹ ਕੁਲ ਦਾ ਮਾਰਗ ਲਿਆ,
ਭੁੱਲੇ ਸ਼ਾਹ ਜੋ ਹਰਿ ਚਿਤ ਲਾਇਆ,
ਹਿੰਦੂ ਨਾ ਨਹੀਂ ਮੁਸਲਮਾਨ.
s2.romanization:
Hindoo naa nahin musalmaan,
baitha trinjan dharam shaalaa.
Sunni naa nahin asaan Shiaa,
sulah kul daa maarag liaa,
Bulleh Shah jo Har chit laaiaa,
Hindoo naa nahin musalmaan.
s2.english_meaning: Neither Hindu nor Muslim: I sit in the spinning-circle of the world, beyond the enclosures of creed. I am neither Sunni nor Shia; I have taken the path of peace with all. Says Bulleh Shah: whoever fixes the heart on the Divine passes beyond these names and divisions.

s2.title: I Need Only One Ranjha
s2.why_dense: This poem turns the Heer-Ranjha romance into a mystical grammar where the beloved is both human and divine. A learner gains access to the central Punjabi metaphor of Ranjha as God and sees how qissa culture enters Sufi lyric.
s2.cultural_signal: Punjabi folk music; Sufi mehfil; widely quoted in heritage literary circles
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਇਕ ਰਾਂਝਾ ਮੈਨੂੰ ਲੋੜੀਦਾ,
ਨਾ ਹੀਰ ਚਾਹੀਦੀ ਹੋਰ।
ਰਾਂਝਾ ਰਾਂਝਾ ਕਰਦੀ ਨੀ ਮੈਂ ਆਪੇ ਰਾਂਝਾ ਹੋਈ,
ਸੱਦੋ ਨੀ ਮੈਨੂੰ ਧੀਦੋ ਰਾਂਝਾ, ਹੀਰ ਨਾ ਆਖੋ ਕੋਈ।
s2.romanization:
ik Raanjha mainoon lorheeda,
naa Heer chaaheedi hor.
Raanjha Raanjha kardi nee main aape Raanjha hoee,
saddo nee mainoon Dheedo Raanjha, Heer naa aakho koee.
s2.english_meaning: I desire only the one Ranjha; I seek no other Heer or worldly counterpart. Repeating the Beloved’s name, I myself am transformed into Ranjha; call me not Heer any longer, for in love the lover is effaced into the one loved.

s2.title: My Heart Dyed in Alif Allah
s2.first_line_gurmukhi: ਅਲਫ਼ ਅੱਲ੍ਹਾ ਨਾਲ ਰੱਤਾ ਦਿਲ ਮੇਰਾ
s2.why_dense: This is a compact lesson in alphabet mysticism, where the first letter becomes the seed of remembrance and inner transformation. A learner gains script-conscious vocabulary and sees how Punjabi verse links sound, letter, and devotion.
s2.cultural_signal: Sufi mehfil; qawwali tradition; Punjabi devotional singing
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਅਲਫ਼ ਅੱਲ੍ਹਾ ਨਾਲ ਰੱਤਾ ਦਿਲ ਮੇਰਾ,
ਮੈਂ ਨੀ ਮੈਂ ਕੌਣ ਆਖਾਂ।
ਬੁੱਲ੍ਹਾ ਕੀ ਜਾਣਾਂ ਮੈਂ ਕੌਣ,
ਨਾ ਮੈਂ ਮੋਮਨ ਵਿਚ ਮਸੀਤਾਂ,
ਨਾ ਮੈਂ ਵਿਚ ਕੁਫ਼ਰ ਦੀਆਂ ਰੀਤਾਂ।
s2.romanization:
Alaf Allah naal rattaa dil mera,
Main nee main kaun aakhaan.
Bullha kee jaana main kaun,
Naa main moman vich maseetaan,
Naa main vich kufar diyaan reetaan.
s2.english_meaning: My heart is dyed in the primal Alif of Allah, and I cannot say what this self is. Bulleh declares that he knows not who he is: neither enclosed among the faithful in mosques, nor bound within the customs of unbelief; the seeker stands beyond both names, colored only by the Divine letter.

s2.title: Love Has Taken Hold
s2.first_line_gurmukhi: ਲਾਗੀ ਰੇ ਲਾਗੀ ਬਲ ਬਲ ਜਾਵੇ
s2.why_dense: This celebrated siharfi shows how alphabetic form can carry the heat of longing rather than mere ornament. A learner gains exposure to the siharfi structure and to the recurring Punjabi Sufi imagery of burning, attachment, and annihilating love.
s2.cultural_signal: Sufi mehfil; Punjabi classical-folk performance; literary recitation
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਲਾਗੀ ਰੇ ਲਾਗੀ ਬਲ ਬਲ ਜਾਵੇ,
ਬਿਨ ਸਾਜਨ ਦੇ ਨੀਂਦ ਨ ਆਵੇ।
ਲਾਗੀ ਰੇ ਲਾਗੀ ਬਲ ਬਲ ਜਾਵੇ,
ਵਿਛੋੜੇ ਵਾਲੀ ਅੱਗ ਸਤਾਵੇ।
s2.romanization:
Laagee re laagee bal bal jaave,
bin saajan de neend na aave.
Laagee re laagee bal bal jaave,
vichhore vaalee agg sataave.
s2.english_meaning: Love has taken hold; I am offered up again and again. Without the Beloved, sleep does not come, and the fire of separation keeps tormenting the heart. The refrain circles like a wound, showing longing as both devotion and burning.

s2.title: Going to Mecca Does Not End the Matter
s2.why_dense: This couplet is a sharp critique of outward pilgrimage without inward awakening, making it one of Bulleh Shah's most portable teachings. A learner gains a memorable proverb-like line and a clear example of how Punjabi Sufi ethics challenge ritualism.
s2.cultural_signal: widely quoted in speech; Sufi mehfil; diaspora social media quotation
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਮੱਕੇ ਗਿਆਂ ਗੱਲ ਮੁਕਦੀ ਨਾਹੀਂ,
ਭਾਵੇਂ ਸੌ ਸੌ ਜੁੰਮੇ ਪੜ੍ਹ ਆਈਏ।
s2.romanization:
Makke giyaan gall mukdi naaheen,
Bhaaven sau sau jumme parh aaaiye.
s2.english_meaning: The matter is not resolved by going to Mecca, even if one returns after offering Friday prayers a hundred times. The outward journey and repeated rites do not complete what remains unfinished within the heart.

s2.title: Your Love Made Me Dance
s2.first_line_gurmukhi: ਤੇਰਾ ਇਸ਼ਕ ਨਚਾਇਆ ਕਰਕੇ ਥਈਆ ਥਈਆ
s2.why_dense: This kafi translates mystical surrender into bodily rhythm, making ecstasy audible and memorable even for new learners. A learner gains high-frequency vocabulary of love and movement through one of the most performed Punjabi Sufi refrains ever recorded.
s2.cultural_signal: qawwali tradition; Punjabi folk music; Sufi mehfil; widely quoted in diaspora
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਤੇਰਾ ਇਸ਼ਕ ਨਚਾਇਆ ਕਰਕੇ ਥਈਆ ਥਈਆ
ਤੇਰਾ ਇਸ਼ਕ ਨੇ ਡੇਰਾ ਮੇਰੇ ਅੰਦਰ ਕੀਤਾ
ਭਰ ਕੇ ਜ਼ਹਰ ਪਿਆਲਾ ਮੈਨੂੰ ਆਪੇ ਪੀਤਾ
ਝਬਦੇ ਬੂਹੇ ਵੇਖੇ, ਖੁਲ੍ਹੇ ਰਹੇ ਨ ਕਿਵਾੜਿਆ
ਤੇਰਾ ਇਸ਼ਕ ਨਚਾਇਆ ਕਰਕੇ ਥਈਆ ਥਈਆ
s2.romanization:
Teraa ishq nachaayaa karke thaiyaa thaiyaa
Teraa ishq ne deraa mere andar keetaa
Bhar ke zahr piyaalaa mainoon aape peetaa
Jhabde boohe vekhe, khulhe rahe na kiwaariyaa
Teraa ishq nachaayaa karke thaiyaa thaiyaa
s2.english_meaning: Your love has made me dance in ecstasy, crying thaiyaa thaiyaa. This love has pitched its camp within me; it filled the cup with poison and made me drink it myself, while the doors I looked toward slammed shut and no shutters remained open. In Bulleh Shah’s image, divine love is both intoxication and annihilation, wounding the self until only the dance remains.

s2.title: Repeating Ranjha, I Became Ranjha
s2.first_line_gurmukhi: ਰਾਂਝਾ ਰਾਂਝਾ ਕਰਦੀ ਨੀ ਮੈਂ ਆਪੇ ਰਾਂਝਾ ਹੋਈ
s2.why_dense: This is one of the clearest Punjabi expressions of fana, where repetition of the beloved's name dissolves the self into the beloved. A learner gains a culturally central line and sees how romance vocabulary becomes a theology of union.
s2.cultural_signal: Punjabi folk music; Sufi mehfil; widely quoted in diaspora; qawwali tradition
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਰਾਂਝਾ ਰਾਂਝਾ ਕਰਦੀ ਨੀ ਮੈਂ ਆਪੇ ਰਾਂਝਾ ਹੋਈ
ਸੱਦੋ ਨੀ ਮੈਨੂੰ ਧੀਦੋ ਰਾਂਝਾ, ਹੀਰ ਨ ਆਖੋ ਕੋਈ
ਰਾਂਝਾ ਮੈਂ ਵਿਚ, ਮੈਂ ਰਾਂਝੇ ਵਿਚ, ਹੋਰ ਖਿਆਲ ਨ ਕੋਈ
ਮੈਂ ਨਹੀਂ ਓਹ ਆਪ ਹੈ, ਆਪਣੀ ਆਪ ਕਰੇ ਦਿਲਜੋਈ
s2.romanization:
Raanjha Raanjha kardi nee main aape Raanjha hoee
Saddo nee mainoon Dheedo Raanjha, Heer na aakho koee
Raanjha main vich, main Raanjhe vich, hor khayaal na koee
Main nahin oh aap hai, aapni aap kare diljoee
s2.english_meaning: Calling “Ranjha, Ranjha,” I myself was transformed into Ranjha; do not call me Heer any longer, but address me as Dheedo Ranjha. The lover and the Beloved have entered one another so completely that no other thought remains; the self is effaced, and only the One remains, consoling the heart by Its own presence.

s2.title: I Am Mad with Love
s2.why_dense: This poem uses the voice of the love-mad woman to normalize spiritual excess and overturn social ideas of sanity. A learner gains colloquial Punjabi emotional register and a key Sufi trope of holy madness that recurs across Punjabi song traditions.
s2.cultural_signal: Punjabi folk music; Sufi mehfil; women's singing repertoires
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਨੀ ਮੈਂ ਕਮਲੀ ਆਂ
ਮੈਂਨੂੰ ਸੱਜਣ ਦੇਖਣ ਦੀ ਲੋੜੀ
ਨੀ ਮੈਂ ਕਮਲੀ ਆਂ
ਬੁੱਲ੍ਹਾ ਸ਼ਹੁ ਦੀ ਸੌਂਹ ਮੈਂਨੂੰ, ਹੋਰ ਨ ਜਾਪੇ ਕੋਈ
s2.romanization:
Nee main kamli aan
Mainoon sajjan dekhan di lorhi
Nee main kamli aan
Bullha Shahu di saunh mainoon, hor na jaape koi
s2.english_meaning: I am mad with love; I ache only for the sight of the Beloved. By Bulleh Shah's solemn oath, no one else appears before me—the whole world fades beside that one vision.

s2.title: The Heart Is Deeper Than the Ocean
s2.why_dense: This maxim compresses an entire inward cosmology into a single image: the heart as a depth beyond measure. A learner gains a proverb-level line still used in speech and a gateway into Punjabi metaphors of darya, depth, and interiority.
s2.cultural_signal: widely quoted in speech; Punjabi music; Sufi mehfil; diaspora citation
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਦਿਲ ਦਰਿਆ ਸਮੁੰਦਰੋਂ ਡੂੰਘੇ, ਕੌਣ ਦਿਲਾਂ ਦੀਆਂ ਜਾਣੇ ਹੋ
ਵਿਚੇ ਬੇੜੀਆਂ ਵਿਚੇ ਜੇੜੇ, ਵਿਚੇ ਵਣ ਮੁਹਾਣੇ ਹੋ
s2.romanization:
dil dariyaa samundaron doonghe, kaun dilaan diyaan jaane ho
viche beriyaan viche jere, viche van muhaane ho
s2.english_meaning: The heart is a river, deeper than the sea; who can truly know the hearts within hearts? Within it are boats, within it are whirlpools and tangled reaches, within it are forests and hidden estuaries—an inward expanse no outward voyager can measure.

s2.title: You Carry Thousands of Books
s2.first_line_gurmukhi: ਪੜ੍ਹ ਪੜ੍ਹ ਇਲਮ ਹਜ਼ਾਰਾਂ ਪੁਸਤਕਾਂ ਨੂੰ ਢੋਂਦਾ ਫਿਰੇਂ
s2.why_dense: This poem attacks bookish learning without self-knowledge, a theme that remains instantly legible to modern students. A learner gains educational and religious vocabulary while meeting a classic Punjabi distinction between outer knowledge and inner realization.
s2.cultural_signal: widely quoted in educational contexts; Sufi mehfil; diaspora discussion circles
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਪੜ੍ਹ ਪੜ੍ਹ ਇਲਮ ਹਜ਼ਾਰਾਂ ਪੁਸਤਕਾਂ ਨੂੰ ਢੋਂਦਾ ਫਿਰੇਂ,
ਕਦੇ ਆਪਣੇ ਆਪ ਨੂੰ ਪੜ੍ਹਿਆ ਹੀ ਨਹੀਂ.
ਜਾ ਜਾ ਵੜਦਾ ਮੰਦਰ ਮਸੀਤੀ,
ਕਦੇ ਮਨ ਆਪਣੇ ਵਿੱਚ ਵੜਿਆ ਹੀ ਨਹੀਂ.
s2.romanization:
Parrh parrhh ilm hazaaran pustakaan noon dhonda phiren,
Kade apne aap noon parrhia hi nahin.
Jaa jaa varda mandar maseeti,
Kade man apne vich varria hi nahin.
s2.english_meaning: You may read and carry thousands of books of learning, yet you have never read your own self. You go again and again into temple and mosque, but have never entered the inner chamber of your own heart.

s2.title: This Was Bound to Happen
s2.why_dense: This short refrain-driven piece teaches surrender without passivity, showing how destiny language can become a mode of trust in the divine. A learner gains idiomatic Punjabi of acceptance and hears a register common in oral performance and everyday speech.
s2.cultural_signal: Punjabi folk music; Sufi mehfil; widely quoted in reflective speech
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਏਹੋ ਹੋਣਾ ਸੀ,
ਏਹੋ ਹੋਣਾ ਸੀ।
ਇਸ਼ਕ ਤੇਰੇ ਨੇ ਮੈਨੂੰ ਥਾਂ ਥਾਂ ਰੁਲਾਇਆ,
ਲੋਕਾਂ ਭਾਣੇ ਚਾਕ ਮਹੀ ਦਾ, ਰਾਂਝਾ ਕਰ ਕੇ ਬਣਾਇਆ,
ਏਹੋ ਹੋਣਾ ਸੀ।
s2.romanization:
Eho honaa see,
Eho honaa see.
Ishq tere ne mainoon thaan thaan rulaayaa,
Lokaan bhaane chaak Mahee da, Raanjhaa kar ke banaayaa,
Eho honaa see.
s2.english_meaning: This indeed was destined to be. Your love has made me wander, cast down from place to place; in the eyes of the world I became the mad lover of my Beloved, fashioned into Ranjha himself. Such was the decree of love, and it could not turn otherwise.

s2.title: Come Before My Eyes, Beloved
s2.why_dense: This lyric makes vision itself the site of longing, joining sensory language to mystical desire in a very teachable way. A learner gains intimate second-person address and the emotional vocabulary of absence, sight, and yearning.
s2.cultural_signal: Punjabi folk music; Sufi mehfil; sung in semi-classical settings
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਅੱਖਾਂ ਓਹਲੇ ਜਾਹ ਨੀ ਯਾਰਾ,
ਅੱਖਾਂ ਓਹਲੇ ਜਾਹ ਨੀ.
ਅੱਖਾਂ ਓਹਲੇ ਜਾਹ ਨੀ ਯਾਰਾ,
ਸਾਡਾ ਦਿਲ ਨਾ ਲੈ ਜਾਹ ਨੀ.
s2.romanization:
Akkhaan ohle jaah nee yaaraa,
akkhaan ohle jaah nee.
Akkhaan ohle jaah nee yaaraa,
saadaa dil naa lai jaah nee.
s2.english_meaning: Do not go out of my sight, beloved; do not vanish from before these eyes. If you must depart, then at least do not carry away my heart, for the lover’s life is bound to the vision of the Friend.

s2.title: Why Go Elsewhere? The Beloved Is Home
s2.first_line_gurmukhi: ਕਿਥੇ ਜਾਇਓ ਬੰਦੇ ਮਾਹੀ ਘਰ ਆਇਓ
s2.why_dense: This poem teaches the Sufi principle of nearness by relocating the search for God from distant travel to the intimate home-space. A learner gains everyday spatial vocabulary and a central Punjabi mystical insight about presence within the ordinary.
s2.cultural_signal: Sufi mehfil; Punjabi devotional singing; widely cited in spiritual talks
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਕਿਥੇ ਜਾਇਓ ਬੰਦੇ ਮਾਹੀ ਘਰ ਆਇਓ
ਸੱਜਣ ਦੇਹਿ ਵਿਹਾਜਿਆ, ਹੋਰ ਨ ਕਿਤੈ ਧਾਇਓ
ਘਰ ਵਿਚ ਰੱਬ ਪਿਆਰਾ ਵੱਸਦਾ, ਬਾਹਰ ਭਾਲ ਨ ਪਾਇਓ
ਬੁੱਲ੍ਹਾ ਸ਼ਾਹ ਸੁਹਾਗਣ ਓਹੀ, ਜੇਹੜੀ ਪਿਆ ਦਾ ਭੇਦ ਪਾਇਓ
s2.romanization:
Kithe jaiyo bande maahi ghar aaiyo
Sajjan dehi vihaajiaa, hor na kitai dhaaiyo
Ghar vich Rabb piaaraa vassdaa, baahar bhaal na paaiyo
Bullha Shah suhaagan ohee, jehri piaa da bhed paaiyo
s2.english_meaning: Why wander elsewhere, O seeker, when the Beloved has come into the home? The Friend has taken up dwelling in the body itself; do not run in other directions, for the Dear One abides within the house of being. Bulleh Shah says: she alone is the true bride who comes to know the secret of the Beloved.

s2.title: What All Are You Saying?
s2.why_dense: This dialogic poem stages argument, bewilderment, and intimacy with the divine in direct spoken Punjabi. A learner gains conversational syntax and sees how Punjabi Sufi poetry often thinks through address rather than abstract exposition.
s2.cultural_signal: Sufi mehfil; literary recitation; heritage Punjabi study circles
s2.full_excerpt_gurmukhi:
ਤੂੰ ਕੀ ਕੀ ਕਹਿੰਦਾ ਏਂ,
ਦਿਲਾਂ ਦਿਆਂ ਜਾਣਦਾ ਏਂ।
ਤੂੰ ਰਾਮ ਕਹੇਂ ਜਾਂ ਰਹੀਮ ਕਹੇਂ,
ਅਸਲ ਨਾਮ ਸਭਨਾਂ ਦਾ ਓਹੀ ਏ।
ਬੁੱਲ੍ਹਾ ਸ਼ਾਹ ਖੜਾ ਏ ਹੈਰਾਨ,
ਤੂੰ ਕੀ ਕੀ ਕਹਿੰਦਾ ਏਂ।
s2.romanization:
toon kee kee kahinda ain,
dilaan diyaan jaanda ain.
toon Raam kahen jaan Raheem kahen,
asal naam sabhnaan da ohee ae.
Bullha Shah kharra ae hairaan,
toon kee kee kahinda ain.
